issue_title: Windows platform dependencies
issue_body:
To successfully install URH in Win7601 (x64) it is also necessary to install the Microsoft Windows SDK for Windows 7 and .NET Framework 3.5 SP1-->Developer Tools > Windows Headers and Libraries and Developer Tools > Visual C++ Compilers.

Without them, the installation of URH on this platform faults with the error: "    c:\program files\python35\include\pyconfig.h(243): fatal error C1083: Cannot
 open include file: 'basetsd.h': No such file or directory
    error: command 'C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Microsoft Visual Studio 14.0\\VC\\B
IN\\x86_amd64\\cl.exe' failed with exit status 2"

From this, it is clear that file, "basetsd.h" is missing.
This file is part of the Windows Headers and Libraries.
And of course if you need that you will need the 64-bit compilers.

## Platform Specifications

  - Python Version: 3.5.2
  - Operating System: Win7601 Ultimate
  - Version of URH: 1.6.4.2

issue_title: Add Etag to cache mechanism
issue_body:
Please refer to [this ccp post](https://developers.eveonline.com/blog/article/esi-etag-best-practices)

> # CACHING CONSIDERATIONS
> ### Things application developers will want to consider:
> 
> 1. Store ETag values with their response data independent of the cache timers (ie; do not expire your data just because the Expires time is in the past)
> 2. Request using the If-None-Match header with the last known ETag as its value
> 3. When you get a 304 response, continue using the last known data. You can use the updated Expires and Date headers to determine when the next possible update will be

issue_title: substitutions result in string which can be a problem for JSON
issue_body:
If using jsonschema on the server side and expecting integers for some values, using a $RESPONSE style substitution in data in a POST or PUT can fail because a value may appear as a string not an int.

Will need to check if the type of the value is preserved and it is just the replace regex that is doing the breakage. If so, it ought to be fixable with relative ease.

issue_title: Inferior fails to launch on openSUSE Leap 42.2 due to libtinfo not providing _nc_outch_sp
issue_body:
##### Describe the issue

On openSUSE Leap 42.2, the inferior debugger fails to launch. The symptom is the following (in the console pane of the gdbgui GUI):

    gdbgui spawned subprocess with pid 21941 from executable gdb.
    gdb process 21941 is running for this tab
    The underlying gdb process has been killed. This tab will no longer function as expected.

This happens every time gdbgui is launched, regardless of arguments.

##### Steps to replicate

* Launch gdbgui on the command line.
* Navigate to 127.0.0.1:5000
* The message(s) appear(s) immediately in the console pane. No debugging is possible.

##### Describe your environment
* Distribution: openSUSE Leap 42.2, 
* `uname -a`: `Linux <hostname> 4.4.104-18.44-default #1 SMP Thu Jan 4 08:07:55 UTC 2018 (05a9de6) x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux`
* gdbgui version (`gdbgui -v`): 0.11.1.2
* gdb version (`gdb -v`): 8.0.1
* browser: tested in Firefox 52.6.0 and Chrome Version 65.0.3325.146 (Official Build). The problem is likely not related to the browser.
* python packages (`pip freeze`): N/A as I'm using the standalone binary.

##### Investigation

`strace`ing the binary reveals the following information. These are the relevant excerpts from the forked process that `exec`s the debugger:

    execve("/usr/bin/gdb", ["/usr/bin/gdb", "--interpreter=mi2"], [<environment info stripped .......>]
    open("/tmp/_MEImWZSco/libncursesw.so.5", O_RDONLY|O_CLOEXEC) = -1 ENOENT (No such file or directory)
    open("/lib64/libncursesw.so.5", O_RDONLY|O_CLOEXEC) = 3
    open("/tmp/_MEImWZSco/libtinfo.so.5", O_RDONLY|O_CLOEXEC) = 3
    writev(2, [{"/usr/bin/gdb", 12}, {": ", 2}, {"symbol lookup error", 19}, {": ", 2}, {"/lib64/libncursesw.so.5", 23}, {": ", 2}, {"undefined symbol: _nc_outch_sp", 30}, {"", 0}, {"", 0}, {"\n", 1}], 10) = 91
    exit_group(127)                         = ?

(I have stripped much for legibility, but I have the full logs available.) Note how it uses the `libncursesw` library from my system, but the `libtinfo` shipped with gdbgui. However, my system `libncursesw` requires the following symbol:

    $ eu-readelf -s /lib64/libncursesw.so.5
    Symbol table [ 4] '.dynsym' contains 661 entries:
     1 local symbol  String table: [ 5] '.dynstr'
      Num:            Value   Size Type    Bind   Vis          Ndx Name
       37: 0000000000000000      0 FUNC    GLOBAL DEFAULT    UNDEF _nc_outch_sp

It is provided by my system's `libtinfo` library:

    $ eu-readelf -s /lib64/libtinfo.so.5
    Symbol table [ 4] '.dynsym' contains 315 entries:
     1 local symbol  String table: [ 5] '.dynstr'
      Num:            Value   Size Type    Bind   Vis          Ndx Name
      206: 000000000001aaf0    187 FUNC    GLOBAL DEFAULT       13 _nc_outch_sp

but not by the one shipped with `gdbgui`, i.e.,

    $ eu-readelf -s /tmp/_MEIQ2ccQj/libtinfo.so.5  | grep _nc_outch_sp

returns nothing.

    $ rpm -qf /lib64/libncursesw.so.5 /lib64/libtinfo.so.5
    libncurses5-5.9-55.9.1.x86_64
    libncurses5-5.9-55.9.1.x86_64

I wasn't able to figure out the version of ncurses that was shipped with gdbgui.

issue_title: Let's Encrypt support
issue_body:
This is in support of twisted/mantissa#40 but the Mantissa component of this is really minimal. Essentially, all Mantissa should be responsible for is providing an object that can:

1. Provide a list of hostnames to respond for.
2. Store a new certificate for a given hostname.
3. Provide a certificate for one of those hostnames on demand.

Interacting with Let's Encrypt requires 1 and 2, serving TLS via txsni requires 3, and the obvious relatedness of these tasks suggests they should be implemented by the same object.
3 is already provided by the `mapping` argument that `SNIMap` takes, so this raises a few questions:

* Should 1-3 all be part of the same interface, or should 1-2 be a separate interface?
* If they're a separate interface, should the interface be defined by txsni at all, or by a separate project?
* Should these be `zope.interface.Interface`s, or just documentation?
* Even if the interface is defined in txsni, should the actual Let's Encrypt implementation be in txsni, or a separate project?

There's no *particular* reason the Let's Encrypt stuff needs to be in txsni itself, that just seems like a convenient place to put it: I guess it really depends on where @glyph wants it. If this does end up in txsni, then I'll break the work up into several branches for the independent bits, so this issue itself is more of a "master tracking issue".

issue_title: Stream reader could defer raising exceptions to end of input buffer
issue_body: Just a thought but you might want to consider allowing the stream reader to defer parsing exceptions rather than raising them immediately to the caller.  The problem with raising the exceptions immediately is that you then drop out and lose the remainder of the stream buffer even though it might contain legitimate data after an item that caused the parser to fail.

issue_title: "Surviving" notation backwards?
issue_body:
Hello. First let me say I'm super excited for this repo! I literally learned about mutation testing today and immediately pip installed your package.

What's confusing me is what you call a "surviving mutant." My expectation was that surviving mutants would be those that still pass the test suite. However, [line 388](https://github.com/boxed/mutmut/blob/c86bde07edaa5340c57b4bb639e7c2f6b7c80a46/mutmut/__main__.py#L388) defines surviving mutants as those that do *not* pass the test suite. This means mutations that aren't a problem wind up being reported as "FAILED," and I haven't found a way to show the mutations that have left the test in a passing state.

TL;DR I want to know which mutants passed my test suite, not which ones don't pass my test suite.

My proposal is to remove `not` from line 388, but this drastically changes the behavior of this code base. Like I said, I'm a mutation testing noob, so it's entirely possible I'm thinking about this the wrong way.

Thanks again for the awesome repo!

issue_title: gems.metaclass relies solely on Python2 metaclass behaviors
issue_body:
Getting an error in Python 3 when running nosetests on `gems` test suite.

```
======================================================================
FAIL: test_docrequire (tests.test_metaclasses.TestDocRequire)
----------------------------------------------------------------------
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/....../gems/tests/test_metaclasses.py", line 25, in test_docrequire
    class A(object):
AssertionError: AssertionError not raised

----------------------------------------------------------------------
```

Seems that the `gems.metaclass.DocRequire` type is relying on Python2 metaclass behaviors:

```python
class A(object):
    __metaclass__ = DocRequire

    def method(self):
        return
```

I believe that the `__metaclass__` attribute is ignored in Python 3.

You will either need to use the [`six` module's metaclass functions](https://stackoverflow.com/a/39013295/1869370) or do some sort of version checking.

issue_title: Credit card numbers' validity is not enforced

Credit card numbers generated by `credit_card_number()` are not always valid.

### Steps to reproduce

```python
fake = Faker()

```

### Expected behavior

The credit card numbers generated should always be valid ones.

### Actual behavior

Some returned numbers are invalid. For example: "3337083863794024", "3337957986872777", "3088184514627042". You can verify it with http://www.validcreditcardnumber.com/

Could the generator be modified to ensure that credit card numbers are always valid?

issue_title: Beginner question for docs: Can "T" do filters and/or grouping?
issue_body:
Hi,

sorry if this is too obvious, but from the tutorial I can't understand if glom supports grouping or if this is not something it's intended to do.

If you extend the planets example to include a category and sum up the moons by that category?

    from glom import glom, T
    target = {'system': {'planets': [{'name': 'earth', 'category':1, 'moons': 1},
        {'name': 'pluto', 'category':1, 'moons': 5},
        {'name': 'uranus', 'category':2, 'moons': 5},
        {'name': 'jupiter', 'category':2, 'moons': 69}]}}

    spec = T['system']['planets']

if I wanted to sum up the moons by category, I could run

    [
        sum([p['moons'] for p in glom(target, spec) if p['category'] == 1]),
        sum([p['moons'] for p in glom(target, spec) if p['category'] == 2])
    ]

But is there a way of adding filtering (for example sum only category 2) or grouping directly to the spec?

Of is this outside the scope of glom?

##UPDATE##

    A spec without the T notation works for filtering:

    spec = ('system.planets', [lambda x : x['moons'] if x['category'] == 2 else OMIT], sum)

    As the T-notation is not fixed yet, I'm closing the issue.

issue_title: Support using same file path for input & output
issue_body:
Sox doesn't support using the same file as both input and output - doing this will result in an empty, invalid audio file. While this is sox behavior and not pysox, it would be nice if pysox took care of this behind the scenes. Right now the user needs to worry about this logic themselves, e.g. like this:

```python
import tempfile
import shutil
from scaper.util import _close_temp_files

audio_infile = '/Users/justin/Downloads/trimtest.wav'
audio_outfile = '/Users/justin/Downloads/trimtest.wav'
start_time = 2
end_time = 3

tfm = sox.Transformer()
tfm.trim(start_time, end_time)
if audio_outfile != audio_infile:
    tfm.build(audio_infile, audio_outfile)
else:
    # must use temp file in order to save to same file
    tmpfiles = []
    with _close_temp_files(tmpfiles):
        # Create tmp file
        tmpfiles.append(
            tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(
                suffix='.wav', delete=True))
        # Save trimmed result to temp file
        tfm.build(audio_infile, tmpfiles[-1].name)
        # Copy result back to original file
        shutil.copyfile(tmpfiles[-1].name, audio_outfile)
```

Pysox *does* issue a warning when a file is about to be overwritten, which is even more confusing under this scenario since the user (who might be unfamiliar with the quirks of sox) has no reason to think that the overwritten file will be invalid.

issue_title: Tests are not showing as "Skipped" when a unittest.SkipTest error is raised inside setUpClass()
issue_body:
## Summary
It appears that green is not handling raised `unittest.SkipTest` exceptions in `setUpClass()` as expected. It's silently skipping the entire class instead of verbosely skipping them.

## Steps to Reproduce
Make this file:
```python
# green_skiptest_error.py
import unittest

class TestMe(unittest.TestCase):
    @classmethod
    def setUpClass(cls):
        raise unittest.SkipTest("Intentionally throwing SkipTest exception")
        pass

    def test_me(self):
        self.assertTrue(True)

    def test_me_too(self):
        self.assertTrue(True)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    unittest.main(verbosity=2)
```

and then run:
```shell
python -m green green_skiptest_error.py -vvv
```


## Actual
```
(.venv-tph-orm) C:\gitlab>python -m green green_skiptest_error.py -vvv
Green 2.8.1, Coverage 4.3.4, Python 3.5.2

Ran 0 tests in 0.427s

No Tests Found
```


## Expected
```shell
(.venv-tph-orm) C:\gitlab>python -m green green_skiptest_error.py -vvv
Green 2.8.1, Coverage 4.3.4, Python 3.5.2

green_skiptest_error
  TestMe
s   test_me -- Intentionally throwing SkipTest exception
s   test_me_too -- Intentionally throwing SkipTest exception

Skipped green_skiptest_error.TestMe.test_me - Intentionally throwing SkipTest exception

Skipped green_skiptest_error.TestMe.test_me_too - Intentionally throwing SkipTest exception

Ran 2 tests in 0.489s

OK (skips=2)
```

#### For comparison, here's the output of `nose2 green_skiptest_error -vvv` and `python green_skiptest_error.py`:
```shell
skipped Intentionally throwing SkipTest exception

----------------------------------------------------------------------
Ran 0 tests in 0.001s

OK (skipped=1)
```

> Note: `nose2` and `unittest` have the same output because `nose2` is simply a test runner and it doesn't modify any output like `green`.

## Versions
Listed in all the code snippets, but here they are anyway:
+ Python 3.5.2 (64-bit) running on WinPython 3.5.2.2
+ Windows 10 Pro AU, 64-bit (v1607, build 14393.1066)
+ green 2.8.1
+ nose2 0.6.5


## Investigation
### If `SkipTest` is raised inside a *test method* rather than the `setUpClass` method, then things work as expected:
```python
import unittest

class TestMe(unittest.TestCase):
    @classmethod
    def setUpClass(cls):
        pass

    def test_me(self):
        raise unittest.SkipTest("Intentionally throwing SkipTest exception")
        self.assertTrue(True)

    def test_me_too(self):
        self.assertTrue(True)
```

```shell
(.venv-tph-orm) C:\gitlab>python -m green green_skiptest_error.py -vvv
Green 2.8.1, Coverage 4.3.4, Python 3.5.2

green_skiptest_error
  TestMe
s   test_me -- Intentionally throwing SkipTest exception
.   test_me_too

Skipped green_skiptest_error.TestMe.test_me - Intentionally throwing SkipTest exception

Ran 2 tests in 0.484s

OK (passes=1, skips=1)
```

### Throwing `SkipTest` inside `setUp` works correctly
```python
import unittest

class TestMe(unittest.TestCase):
    def setUp(self):
        raise unittest.SkipTest("Intentionally throwing SkipTest exception")
        pass

    def test_me(self):
        self.assertTrue(True)

    def test_me_too(self):
        self.assertTrue(True)
```

```shell
(.venv-tph-orm) C:\gitlab>python -m green green_skiptest_error.py -vvv
Green 2.8.1, Coverage 4.3.4, Python 3.5.2

green_skiptest_error
  TestMe
s   test_me -- Intentionally throwing SkipTest exception
s   test_me_too -- Intentionally throwing SkipTest exception

Skipped green_skiptest_error.TestMe.test_me - Intentionally throwing SkipTest exception

Skipped green_skiptest_error.TestMe.test_me_too - Intentionally throwing SkipTest exception

Ran 2 tests in 0.491s

OK (skips=2)
```


## Comments
When an entire class is skipped, the skip message should be listed on the class output *and* the test output. Example (colors are simply listed in square brackets at the end of the line):
```shell
(.venv-tph-orm) C:\gitlab>python -m green green_skiptest_error.py -vvv
Green 2.8.1, Coverage 4.3.4, Python 3.5.2

green_skiptest_error
  TestMe -- [Skipped] Intentionally throwing SkipTest exception     [cyan/blue]
s   test_me -- Intentionally throwing SkipTest exception            [cyan/blue]
s   test_me_too -- Intentionally throwing SkipTest exception        [cyan/blue]

Skipped green_skiptest_error.TestMe.test_me - Intentionally throwing SkipTest exception

Skipped green_skiptest_error.TestMe.test_me_too - Intentionally throwing SkipTest exception

Ran 2 tests in 0.491s

OK (skips=2)
```

(However, this probably should be a separate issue/feature request)

issue_title: SNMPV3 3DES privacy does not work.
issue_body:
SNMPV3 3DES privacy does not work. This is because the key localization done in the
current version follows  https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-blumenthal-aes-usm-04 which
is for AES only. Key localization should be done as per  https://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-reeder-snmpv3-usm-3desede-00 
Also, the current encrypt/decrypt methods are doing extra, unnecessary work.
All they need to do is use the encrypt/decrypt methods provided by the 3DES object from Pycrypto.

Please see the pull request that fixes this issue.

issue_title: xvfb-run failed to start
issue_body:
After using this package, I have run into an issue with the invocation of xvfb-run under the hood. Each virtual headless server xvfb-run spins up will have spin down time. My script calls imgkit synchronously with repetitious invocations of the xvfb-run. The default server it tries is always the same (:99) of which each new one tries this server and fails as the process is still open in memory. A sleep of 100MS has been the work around.

The solution would be to tell xvfb-run with the option `-a, --auto-servernum Try to get a free server number, starting at 99, or the argument to --server-num.` 

Is there a way currently to pass this option down from imgkit to the xvfb-run process being called?

If not, I will submit a PR of this possibility.

issue_title: Just Curious: Why so slow?
issue_body:
As a precursor to my question: I'm not here to cast doubt upon the usefulness of this project or the adequacy of those who have worked on it. In truth I am very impressed by this project and when I first discovered it I began using it in most of my projects! (I do lots of data mining, so a library such as this is priceless.)

But I noticed recently that some of the functions in Pydash are very slow compared to their Python counterparts. For instance consider the following code: 

```
from pydash import filter_

In [.]: x = [{'a': 1, 'b': 2, 'c': 3}, {'a': 2, 'b': 3, 'c': 4}]
In [.]: %timeit filter_(x, {'a': 1})
10000 loops, best of 3: 62.1 µs per loop

In [.]: %timeit filter(lambda y: y['a'] is 1, x)
1000000 loops, best of 3: 417 ns per loop
``` 

Now in truth I would never expect the Pydash equivalent functions to be *faster* than the Python counterpart, but *~150x slower*?

Any idea how the speed of these functions could be improved? Since I made this performance discovery I've stopped using the pydash library so much and went back to filter + lambda...but goodness that can get ugly! I love the idea of this library, I just wished performance was better.

issue_title: Modal dialogs stops the asyncio event loop
issue_body:
Here is a simple test file : 

```python
import sys
import unittest
import os
import asyncio
import quamash
import logging
from PyQt5.QtWidgets import QDialog, QMessageBox
from PyQt5.QtCore import Qt
from PyQt5.QtTest import QTest


class Dialog(QDialog):
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()

    @asyncio.coroutine
    def message_box(self):
        result = QMessageBox.question(self, "Test", "Do I bug ?")
        return result


class TestBug(unittest.TestCase):
    def setUp(self):
        self.qapplication = quamash.QApplication(sys.argv)
        self.lp = quamash.QEventLoop(self.qapplication)
        asyncio.set_event_loop(self.lp)

    def tearDown(self):
        try:
            self.lp.close()
        finally:
            asyncio.set_event_loop(None)

    def test_bug(self):
        dialog = Dialog()
        @asyncio.coroutine
        def exec_test():
            asyncio.async(dialog.message_box())
            yield from asyncio.sleep(3)
            for widget in quamash.QApplication.topLevelWidgets():
                if isinstance(widget, QMessageBox):
                    QTest.mouseClick(widget.button(QMessageBox.Yes), Qt.LeftButton)
            yield from asyncio.sleep(1)

        self.lp.run_until_complete(asyncio.wait_for(exec_test(), timeout=10))

if __name__ == '__main__':
    logging.basicConfig( stream=sys.stderr )
    logging.getLogger().setLevel( logging.DEBUG )
    unittest.main()
```

This outputs : 
```
/usr/bin/python3.4 /home/inso/bin/pycharm-4.5.4/helpers/pycharm/utrunner.py /home/inso/code/ucoin/cutecoin/src/cutecoin/tests/process_cfg_community/test_bug.py true

issue_title: Error in transformation of label coordinates
issue_body:
In the definition of CellReference, polygons are (correctly) transformed according to 
```
polygons[kk][ii] = polygons[kk][ii] * ct \
                                + polygons[kk][ii][:, ::-1] * st
```
(e.g. x<- cos(theta) x - sin(theta) y, but labels are transformed according to:
```
lbl.position = lbl.position * ct + lbl.position * st
```
(e.g. x<- cos(theta) x - sin(theta) x), which is not consistent or unitary.  The label formula should be corrected to follow the same formula as the polygons.  
```
lbl.position = lbl.position * ct + lbl.position[::-1] * st
```
This correction fixed the disagreement I was seeing with a layout viewed in klayout.

issue_title: rewinding a cassette
issue_body: Would it be possible to add a ``rewind()`` function to the cassette by resetting the ``play_counts`` counter?  This would allow replaying the same request multiple times on a cassette. I've implemented this myself but would be interested in adding it to the cassette class itself.

issue_title: delay factor for getting prompt too small
issue_body:
I ran into some issue to get the prompt for hp switches. After I looked into the code for hp_procurve_ssh.py and base_connection.py and found out the reason. Even the code compared the global delay and default delay factor, but the delay is not passed to set_base_prompt() in hp_procurve_ssh.py. In the set_base_prompt(), it can only use the default value,which is 1 sec, as the parameter, and it only waits 0.1 seconds to get the prompt. In my case, the device takes a little longer than 0.1 to read the prompt. 

we can either change .1 to .5 or 1 to wait longer for get the prompt, or passing the delay to set_base_prompt after select_delay_factor.

Another thing I don't quite understand is why the select_delay_factor is not implemented in base_connection. it sounds like a basic feature for all kinds of devices

issue_title: PEP8 Compliance
issue_body:
PEP8 support is always a very nice-to-have, especially with a project receiving contributions. Fortunately `netmiko` is almost there with the following exceptions:

Flag | Issue
-------|--------
E303 | Too many blank lines
E501 | Line length > 79
F401 | Module imported, but never used

Looking at errors for the E303, it's because methods are consistently double-spaced where they should be single spaced within a class (global functions are double spaced).

E501 wouldn't be too tough to fix, a lot of what I saw was pretty straight forward.

F401 is a simple fix. It complains because `__init__.py` imports modules to be exported, but is never used beyond that. Since this is for the purpose of exporting, `__all__` should be set to a list containing these objects.

I can help/fix these if there's interest in making it conform to Python stylistic standards. :)

issue_title: conf_changed can happen before bootstrap
issue_body: While its still reading the config, it looks like `TorConfig` can get `CONF_CHANGED` events (and probably does bad things). It should ignore `CONF_CHANGED` until after bootstrap is completed.

issue_title: Retrieve Multiple fields at a time.
issue_body:
Hi Team,

This issue is raised as part of a question from [stack overflow](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/49678099/match-multiple-keys-values-to-database-entry-in-tinydb/49679044#49679044) .

Assume we have a following table, 
```
HHK_STAFF = DB.table("HHK_STAFF")

HHK_STAFF.insert({'EMPLOYEE_ID':'00000001', 
                                 'FNAME': 'Some', 'LNAME':'Person', 
                                 'MNAME':'Random'})
```

Suppose if we need to check a given FNAME, LNAME, MNAME exists, we need to write 

```
def is_user_exists(first_name, middle_name, last_name):
    if HHK_STAFF.search(where("FNAME") == first_name
                                & where("MNAME") == middle_name
                                & where("LNAME") == last_name):
               # user exists
    else:
               # user not eixsts
```

Instead of this is it possible query multiple field like this and perform the comparison?

```
def is_user_exists(first_name, middle_name, last_name): 
    if HHK_STAFF.search(where(("FNAME", "MNAME", "LNAME")) == (FNAME, MNAME, LNAME))):
               # user exists
    else:
               # user not eixsts
```

issue_title: Parsing bug on anyof and allof
Possible to change license to LGPL3?
issue_body:
IANAL but...

As it stands, any software that includes this library will be forced to be GPL3, which makes it incompatible with many standard python libraries (usually non-copyleft licenses like MIT or BSD), and certainly unusable by any company that might want to benefit from it.

LGPL would keep this specific code copyleft but allow the library to be linked by other code bases without compromising those licenses.

issue_title: Segmentation evaluation - boundary detection: to trim or not to trim / special segment labels
issue_body:
Quite frequently, ground-truth annotations contain leading and/or trailing silence, typically (e.g. in SALAMI) annotated using special segment labels like 'Silence'. When such a special segment label appears at the front, the start time of this segment does not contain a real boundary which boundary detection algorithms would find, conversely for ending times of silent end segments. 
Currently, it seems that the segment.detection function must be tricked by introducing very short silent segments, if those are not already present in the ground-truth, to consistently trim them, or not.

issue_title: Not possible to serialize the AST
issue_body:
`pickle.dump` can't handle ast because the `ast` class uses `__slots__` but doesn't implement `__getstate__`. 

It'd be useful to add that functionality. Use case: parse a large repository once and store the ast for further processing. This way the source tree is parsed only once, saving time.

It appears to be an instance of:

- [issue 309 of `setuptools`](https://bitbucket.org/pypa/setuptools/issues/309/error-each-element-of-ext_modules-option)
- the discussion over here: http://stackoverflow.com/questions/21594925/error-each-element-of-ext-modules-option-must-be-an-extension-instance-or-2-t

and possibly related to:

- [issue 275 of `distribute`](https://bitbucket.org/tarek/distribute/issues/275/cannot-specify-cython-under-setup_requires)
- [issue 288 of `setuptools`](https://bitbucket.org/pypa/setuptools/issues/288/cannot-specify-cython-under-setup_requires)

It arose when testing `dd` [on Travis](https://travis-ci.org/johnyf/dd/jobs/100388826).
There is no other workaround that I expect will work for `dd`, other than to either:

- avoid this from happening in `psutil`
- pursue a fix in `setuptools` or `cython` (not sure whose the culprit -- could be `distribute`, but not expected to make a fix available soon)
- drop `psutil` from `install_requires` of `dd`.

issue_title: open_files() on Linux may erroneously raise NoSuchProcess
issue_body:
When iterating over `/proc/{pid}/fdinfo/{fd}` "fd" may disappear on us. In this case psutil treats this condition as "process is gone" and raises NoSuchProcess.
Instead, it should skip the fd and go on.

issue_title: Need examplse of Action.warn etc.
issue_body:
I'm trying to refactor some code that uses the standard `logging.getLogger` to use `lithoxyl` and I'm a bit stuck without some more examples comparing the old style with the new.

In particular, how should I go about converting code that looks like:

```
logger.info('starting some process')
logger.debug('intermediate result %d', a)
logger.debug('another intermediate result %s', b)
logger.info('complete')
```

Logging the start/finish is fine,  but I'm stuck on the "right" way to log the intermediate results. Should they just be assigned to Action's data:

```
with app_log.info('process') as act:
    act['intermediate result'] = a
    act.success()
```

That feels a bit clunky as the resulting `repr` of the data in the log line is pretty unreadable and out of order.

Or can `warn` be used? It doesn't seem quite like it does what I want as it has a different level than the `Action` and it's not outputting how I would expect.

```
with app_log.info('process') as act:
    act.warn('intermediate result %d', a)
    act.success()
```

issue_title: Year-match isn't treated equally among providers
issue_body:
Hey,

When comparing the match determination of OpenSubtitles to other providers, there seems to be a huge difference: OpenSubtitles provider only adds the year match to the matches list [if the video had a year (bool(video.year)) and both the video and the subtitle have the same year set](https://github.com/Diaoul/subliminal/blob/develop/subliminal/providers/opensubtitles.py#L76)  (and it's a movie).

In comparison to that, [let's say Addic7ed](https://github.com/Diaoul/subliminal/blob/develop/subliminal/providers/addic7ed.py#L55): It blindly compares the value of `video.year` with the value of `subtitle.year` - which adds `year` to the matches list, if both values are None.

~~If I'm not wrong, this results in a pretty distinct behaviour: All other providers besides OpenSubtitles match 44 points too high - or OpenSubtitles generally matches 44 points too low, if the year isn't set on the video.~~
Edit: not really, because Addic7ed only matches for TV series. Still, when I look at podnapisi, the year attribute isn't checked at all and doesn't get added to the matches list even for movies.

I'd expect consistent behaviour, and I think the OpenSubtitles provider is wrong, because a None-year should match if both the video and the subtitles have it.

issue_title: SASL_PLAINTEXT authentication failure, doesn't even connect to the Kerberos server
issue_body:
I'm trying to establish a SASL_PLAINTEXT connection to Kafka via kafka-python.

I am providing the necessary arguments listed in the documents:

    {
     'sasl_mechanism': 'PLAIN',
     'sasl_plain_username': 'user',
     'sasl_plain_password': 'secret',
     'security_protocol': 'SASL_PLAINTEXT'
    }

However, it always fails with the following error:

    File "/home/adam/j/kafka/.venv/lib/python3.4/site-packages/kafka/conn.py", line 461, in _handle_sasl_handshake_response
      return self._try_authenticate_plain(future)
    File "/home/adam/j/kafka/.venv/lib/python3.4/site-packages/kafka/conn.py", line 495, in _try_authenticate_plain
      raise error
    kafka.errors.AuthenticationFailedError: AuthenticationFailedError: Authentication failed for user user

I checked the Kerberos server logs, and it doesn't even register a connection attempt when this error happens. I also checked the code [around where the fatal exception occurs](https://github.com/dpkp/kafka-python/blob/master/kafka/conn.py#L484), and it seems to be communicating with a socket on the *Kafka* server, rather than the *Kerberos* server.

Now I'm no Kerberos expert, but since I'm trying to authenticate via Kerberos, shouldn't this part of the code connect to the Kerberos server?

In fact, how does it even know where the Kerberos server is?  When I (successfully) use the CLI tools to connect to Kafka with SASL, I must provide a `java.security.krb5.conf` file, which contains the network addresses of the `kdc` and `admin_server`. How do I provide this information to kafka-python?

issue_title: [Django] Third-party library missing a file triggers a `MissingFileError`
issue_body:
# Intro

My Django project relies on a few third-party JS/CSS libraries that get installed via Bower.

One of those libraries refers to a static file that happens not to be included in the library itself. This triggers a `MissingFileError` when `collectstatic` is run.

# Details

While the missing file is clearly a bug in the JS/CSS library I was wondering if there can be a way this can be addressed on the WhiteNoise side. It is also important to note that the missing image is actually an image in the *documentation* of the library, and is not part of the actual JS/CSS core.

```

The CSS file 'js/lib/bower_components/vis/docs/css/tipuesearch.css' references a file which could not be found:

NB: Compression is enabled in my setup, with `STATICFILES_STORAGE = 'whitenoise.storage.CompressedManifestStaticFilesStorage'`.

issue_title: Python 3: Hidden AttributeError
issue_body:
Hi, first I'd like to thank you for creating such an useful tool for interactive dot viewing! My first use of this tool was 6 years ago, to view the control flow graphs generated from some compilers.

Today I encountered a small logical bug at https://github.com/jrfonseca/xdot.py/blob/427cd4b/xdot/ui/window.py#L112, where it sets `error` to be a `str` while in the other branch (on L115) `error` is set to be a `bytes` (as no `encoding` or `errors` is set in `subprocess.Popen` above). Then on L118 there's a `error.decode()` which is only valid for `str` but not for `bytes`, which in my case triggers this bug.

One way to fix this is just to move the `decode` statement to the branch on L115.

issue_title: to child() or not
issue_body:
With #9 we can more easily pass in variables for paths and let pyrebase build that path. Given that queries are now more aligned with the javascript library, would it also make sense to chain child paths in a similar way? e.g. ```python admin.query("users").child("Marty").get()```

The benefit is that it is again more explicit. Additionally, if a user tries out pyrebase and are familiar with the javascript way of handling firebase then it might be easier for them to get started because it fits their mental model. 

So in the end you could build paths three way:
 ```python admin.query("users/Marty").get()```
 ```python admin.query("users", "Marty").get()```
 ```python admin.query("users").child("Marty").get()```

Is this flexible or confusing? Not sure yet.

issue_title: Make documentation searchable using GitHub's code search
issue_body:
What if we kept a copy of wiki pages in repo's root directory in "wiki.copy". This would make wiki pages easily searchable using code searching function on GitHub. A good idea or a bad idea? Minus: this would create duplicate content - it would require to keep the copy of the wiki synchronized with the real wiki. Plus: search everything (code and documentation) from one place.

How about a more drastic step - get rid of real wiki and put it in a "wiki" directory in repository. Also create Wiki.md page next to README.md and put the Home page wiki content there. Minus: we would lose the Home TOC sidebar. We already use tools to add TOC using DocToc. We can create "wikidocs.py" script that will cal DocToc and also do some other enhancements to wiki pages - maybe add navigation bar "You are here: Wiki Pages Home > API". We could also add the missing Home TOC sidebar with some custom HTML in markdown code.

Real wiki pages would have for sure better Google rating, as they have less nesting, compare:

```
https://github.com/cztomczak/cefpython/wiki/API
vs
https://github.com/cztomczak/cefpython/blob/master/wiki/API.md
```

Searching everything from one place would be a power feature. When I do a modification in code I can easily search for any references in documentation and update it right away.

You can search markdown only pages using [this link](https://github.com/cztomczak/cefpython/search?l=markdown&q=&utf8=%E2%9C%93) or by selecting "Markdown" language in the left side bar on the code search page.

Keeping docs in repo would allow users for easy update of documenation within pull requests.

Structure of the docs/ directory:

```
build/
download/
handlers/
misc/
modules/
objects/
settings/

API.md
KnowledgeBase.md
...
```

issue_title: Mimesis Simple HTTP Server
issue_body:
Implement very simple http server like in module `http.server`, which returns generated data by mimesis.

The idea is to pass template in header, and ignore body and query arguments, for future integration with another server.

#### For example:

Template:
```json
{
  "id": ":uuid",
  "version": "0.1.2",
  "owner.full_name": ":name :personal.surname",
  "owner.email": {"_type": "str", "_field": ":email", "_key": "lower"},
  "owner.phones": {"_type": "list", "_count": 5, "_field": [":telephone", "other1", "other2"]}
}
```

Output:
```json
{
  "id": "dfcfd2f3-0799-b519-1a94-326c978b10c6",
  "version": "0.1.2",
  "owner": {
    "full_name": "Charley Parker",
    "email": "cheetah1869@yandex.com",
    "phones": ["776.907.0588", "717.592.4382", "168.028.7210", "other1", "other2"]
  }
}
```
> Or we can implement something like jinja2 templating system for open customize output

Some header examples:

- **Mimesis-Template** - Template for response
- **Mimesis-Count** - Count of outputs. Default: 0
  - 0 - Response contain object.
  - 1-99 - Response contain array of objects.
- **Mimesis-Locale** - Response locale. Default: en

It's may be helpful for prototyping some RESTful/GRAPHql api.

issue_title: Using SQLAlchemy data stored as binary and wrapped as binary when opened
issue_body:
Python 3.5 + MySQL

When data is stored to a session it is pickled and stored as a binary string:

ex:
   
    session["card"] = "'PP000007C|'
    # pickled as:
    b'\x80\x03}q\x00(X\x06\x00\x00\x00_freshq\x01\x89X\x05\x00\x00\x00cardsq\x02X\n\x00\x00\x00PP000007C|q\x03u.'

When we later try to get the data, it is pulled as a bytes object out of the database and wrapped again as a string:

For example on that same string above it is pulled out of the database as:
        `b"b'\x80\x03}q\x00(X\x06\x00\x00\x00_freshq\x01\x89X\x05\x00\x00\x00cardsq\x02X\n\x00\x00\x00PP000007C|q\x03u.'"`

As you can see, the binary string is stored again cast as a binary string. 

Relevant files:
flask_kvsession\__init__.py
Line 186:

    # save the session, now its no longer new (or modified)
    data = self.serialization_method.dumps(dict(session))

`# in this case, data == b'\x80\x03}q\x00(X\x06\x00\x00\x00_freshq\x01\x89X\x05\x00\x00\x00cardsq\x02X\n\x00\x00\x00PP000007C|q\x03u.'`

JJ

issue_title: start date larger than end date when get pyfolio analzyer for multi-timeframe strategy
issue_body:
Here's the test to re produce this bug:

```py
import backtrader as bt
import backtrader.feeds as btfeeds
import pandas as pd

def test_2_timeframe_pyfolio_positions():
    class SimplePandasData(btfeeds.PandasData):
        params = dict(
            high=None,
            low=None,
            open=None,
            volume=None,
            openinterest=None,
        )

    index = pd.date_range('2017-01-01 09:01',
                          '2017-01-04 09:01',
                          freq='1 Min')
    data_5m = pd.DataFrame({'close':range(1000, 1000+len(index))},
                           index=index)

    index = pd.date_range('2017-01-01',
                          '2017-01-04',
                          freq='1 D')

    data_1d = pd.DataFrame({'close':range(10000, 10000+len(index))},
                           index=index)

    class S(bt.Strategy):
        def __init__(self):
            self.order = None

        def next(self):
            if not self.order:
                self.order = self.buy()

    c = bt.Cerebro()
    c.addstrategy(S)
    c.addanalyzer(bt.analyzers.PyFolio, _name='pyfolio')

    c.adddata(SimplePandasData(dataname=data_5m))
    c.adddata(SimplePandasData(dataname=data_1d))

    results = c.run()
    strat = results[0]
    pyfoliozer = strat.analyzers.getbyname('pyfolio')
    returns, positions, transactions, gross_lev = pyfoliozer.get_pf_items()

    # error here:
    # positions end date should be no smaller than start date
    assert positions.index[-1] >= positions.index[0]
```

I notice, however, if the second daily timeframe data starts not before the mintute timeframe data, then there's no error:

```py
def test_2_timeframe_pyfolio_positions():
    class SimplePandasData(btfeeds.PandasData):
        params = dict(
            high=None,
            low=None,
            open=None,
            volume=None,
            openinterest=None,
        )

    index = pd.date_range('2017-01-01 09:01',
                          '2017-01-04 09:01',
                          freq='1 Min')
    data_5m = pd.DataFrame({'close':range(1000, 1000+len(index))},
                           index=index)

    index = pd.date_range('2017-01-01 09:01', # here we start from min date's begin time
                          '2017-01-04',
                          freq='1 D')

    data_1d = pd.DataFrame({'close':range(10000, 10000+len(index))},
                           index=index)

    class S(bt.Strategy):
        def __init__(self):
            self.order = None

        def next(self):
            if not self.order:
                self.order = self.buy()

    c = bt.Cerebro()
    c.addstrategy(S)
    c.addanalyzer(bt.analyzers.PyFolio, _name='pyfolio')

    c.adddata(SimplePandasData(dataname=data_5m))
    c.adddata(SimplePandasData(dataname=data_1d))

    results = c.run()
    strat = results[0]
    pyfoliozer = strat.analyzers.getbyname('pyfolio')
    returns, positions, transactions, gross_lev = pyfoliozer.get_pf_items()

    # there's no error now
    # positions end date should be no smaller than start date
    assert positions.index[-1] >= positions.index[0]
```

If you need more info just comment to let me know.

issue_title: Links to URLs ending in brackets are truncated
issue_body:
URLs like `https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Set_(mathematics)` which end with a bracket seem to get truncated by Mistletoe. It seems to grab a URL by searching for the first `)` character, omitting brackets and any following characters. For instance: ``` $ mistletoe mistletoe [version 0.5.4] (interactive) Type Ctrl-D to complete input, or Ctrl-C to exit. >>> [link](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Set_(mathematics)) ...

link)

``` I'm not sure if this is a serious issue---after all, we can encode the bracket in the URL as `%29` and all is well. For what it's worth, GitHub's flavour of markdown seems to be happy with URLs ending in (at least one) bracket. The string `[link](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Set_(mathematics))` results in [link](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Set_(mathematics)), and all is well.

issue_title: Hypothesis doesn't pick up on nose's yield statements
Make Simulator module in analogous fashion to Fitter
issue_body:
The way we currently simulate images is a bit dodgy - you can't access stuff like the noise properly and don't have a great deal of customization. Furthermore, the generation of the image from a tracer is handled separately.

I believe we can make a much better simulation module by structuring it in the same way that Fitter is now structured. Specifically, we can perform all processing (in the correct order) in the class constructor (e.g. simulate image_plane_image -> blur with psf -> add sky -> add noise) and then access the different simulated quantities via properties which are automatically mapped to 2D. This will allow us to turn different features on and off without need copious amounts of if loops.

issue_title: Section needs to know its alias
Overriden methods of C/C++ extensions
issue_body:
Originally reported by: **Florian Bruhin (Bitbucket: [The-Compiler](https://bitbucket.org/The-Compiler), GitHub: [The-Compiler](https://github.com/The-Compiler))**

----------------------------------------

When subclassing a method of a class defined in a C/C++ extension, such as this example using the PyQt GUI framework:

```python
from PyQt5.QtCore import QSize
from PyQt5.QtWidgets import QLineEdit, QApplication


class LineEdit(QLineEdit):

    def sizeHint(self):
        return QSize(128, 128)

app = QApplication([])
le = LineEdit()
le.show()
app.exec_()
```

vulture doesn't detect that `sizeHint` is called via C++:

```
foo.py:7: Unused function 'sizeHint'
```

Maybe if a class inherits from a non-Python class, and an existing method is overridden, it should always be considered used?

----------------------------------------
- Bitbucket: https://bitbucket.org/jendrikseipp/vulture/issue/8

issue_title: Issue when 'equal to' conditional formatting is used with strings
issue_body:
Hi,

I am using XlsxWriter to write Excel files with conditional formatting. The problem I am encountering is that an equals sign is always appended to the value which gets inserted into the conditional formatting configuration in my Excel workbook. This causes text-based conditional formatting rules to not evaluate correctly. Numeric-based conditional formatting rules do not appear to be affected.

I am using Python version 3.6, XlsxWriter 0.9.9, and Excel 2016.

Here is some code that demonstrates the problem:

```python
import xlsxwriter

workbook = xlsxwriter.Workbook('XLSX Writer Test.xlsx')
worksheet = workbook.add_worksheet()

worksheet.write('A1', 'X')

format1 = workbook.add_format({'bg_color': '#FFC7CE',
                               'font_color': '#9C0006'})

worksheet.conditional_format('A1', {'type':     'cell',
                                    'criteria': 'equal to',
                                    'value':    'X',
                                    'format':   format1})
workbook.close()
```

After running the above code:
1. Open the Excel file
2. Select cell A1
3. On the Home ribbon, click on Conditional Formatting
4. Double-click on the conditional formatting rule
5. Observe that the value (which was passed as 'X' in the code above) is recorded as '=X', which causes the rule to not execute correctly

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/25124961/43841007-bfe6f8bc-9ae7-11e8-9b6a-f54963e6c4e0.png)

Thanks!

issue_title: Peer discovery for Electron Cash (BCH) servers
issue_body:
I'm looking into running a server for Electron Cash. I'm using a Bitcoin ABC full node and simply plugging it into ElectrumX (as others seem to have done as well).

One issue I am looking at, is peer discovery. For now, I'm planning to simply disable it (non-BCH electrum nodes should reject my node anyway). However, it does give advantages to use it.

According to the [documentation about peer discovery](https://github.com/kyuupichan/electrumx/blob/master/docs/PEER_DISCOVERY.rst#serverfeatures-rpc-call), the hash for the genesis block is used for peer discovery. Which is the same for both networks. Making it not very useful to quickly dismiss wrong peers.

Am I correct that peer discovery wouldn't work correctly for servers running on bitcoin cash? And would there be a way to enable this that doesn't clash with normal nodes?

issue_title: client hangs while running
issue_body:
I'm using Python 2.7.12, iperf 3.0.11 on a Ubuntu 16.04.1 x86_64 machine.

I configured the client to run as follows:

```
>>> import iperf3
>>> client = iperf3.Client()
>>> client.duration = 125
>>> client.bind_address = '127.0.0.1'
>>> client.server_hostname = '127.0.0.1'
>>> client.port = 9923
>>> client.bandwidth = (5096 * 1024)
>>> client.num_streams = 3
>>> client.protocol = "tcp"
>>> client.bulksize = (client.bandwidth * client.duration)/8
>>> client.run()
```

The bandwidth parameter is configured that way since I need 5096 kbps. issue_title: WeakFunctionProxy bypasses wrapt.decorator
issue_body:
Thank you for putting in the effort to write this package and explaining all the issues it addresses.  My decorators would be much more fragile without your help!

WeakFunctionProxy only proxies the original function, and ignores any patching done via wrapt.decorator.  Ex (tested in python 2.7 with wrapt 1.10.4):

```
import wrapt

@wrapt.decorator
def bark(wrapped, instance, args, kwargs):
    print 'The %s barks' % instance.__class__.__name__

class Animal(object):
    @bark
    def squeal(self):
        print 'The %s squeals' % self.__class__.__name__

animal = Animal()
animal.squeal()
```
Output: `The Animal barks`
```
weak_squeal = wrapt.WeakFunctionProxy(animal.squeal)
weak_squeal()
```
Output: `The Animal squeals`

issue_title: Designspace needs to store information about rules
issue_body:
This is a proposal for some xml to record conditional glyph substitutions in designspace.

- I'm not sure if this covers all the conditions allowed by OpenType 1.8
- this is not proposal for feature language.
- just covers single glyphs, not groups. 
- does not cover GPOS-like things. 

```
<!-- optional: list of substitution rules -->
	<rules>
		<rule name="vertical bars" enabled="true">
			<sub name="cent" byname="cent.alt"/>
			<sub name="dollar" byname="dollar.alt"/>
			<condition tag="wght" operator=">" value="250.000000"/>
			<condition tag="wdth" operator="<" value="100"/>
			<condition tag="opsz" operator=">" value="40"/>
		</rule>
    </rules>
```
## rules
`rules` element: groups all the rules. Contains one or more `rule` elements

## rule
`rule` element: a single rule. Contains one or more `sub` elements, and one or more `condition` elements. All conditions must evaluate to `true` for the rule to be
-  `name`: attribute, optional. String. Descriptive name for this rule.
- `enabled`: attribute, optional. Bool. If this rule should be evaluated or not.

## sub
`sub` element: a single glyph to glyph substitution. 
- `name` attribute, required. Name of the source glyph. 
- `byname` attribute, required. Name of the replacement glyph.

## condition
`condition` element: a single condition that has to evaluate to true for this rule to be valid.
- `tag` element: attribute, required. OpenType axis tag. 
- `operator` attribute, required. Can be either < or >
- `value` attribute, required. Number.

issue_title: pause & resume failure: AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'timezone'
issue_body:
APScheduler-3.0.5

This seems to be located in  _lookup_job in schedulers/base.py

Once I have my scheduler, I'm then getting the job object for a particular job:
jobObject = scheduler.get_job(jobID)

When I pause the job using jobObject.pause(), an "AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'timezone'" is produced inside apscheduler/job.py/_modify():

AttributeError: "'NoneType' object has no attribute 'timezone'"
-> apscheduler/job.py(192)_modify()
-> approved['next_run_time'] = convert_to_datetime(value, self._scheduler.timezone, 'next_run_time')

Even though I'm passing a jobObject where I have actually set the _scheduler manually, the Job class in turn calls the the scheduler to pause_job, which then inside modify_job it again calls _lookup_job and gets a new Job object but the _scheduler is set to None, which then fails inside job._modify().

One solution is inside of schedulers/base.py set the scheduler before the Job object is returned.

        for job, alias, replace_existing in self._pending_jobs:
            if job.id == job_id:
                job._scheduler = self
                return job, None

        # Look in all job stores
        for alias, store in six.iteritems(self._jobstores):
            if jobstore_alias in (None, alias):
                job = store.lookup_job(job_id)
                if job is not None:
                    job._scheduler = self
                    return job, alias



The solution above seems to work with the current code, but may not elegant... A better solution might be to figure out how to not have to call lookup job again. Job.pause() calls the scheduler with just the job id, so all that information is already available

I've had very good experiences this past year with Jacques de Hooge's Transcrypt Python to JS transpiler.  http://www.transcrypt.org/ and https://github.com/QQuick/Transcrypt

Mike

issue_title: PatternMatchingEventHandler behavior
issue_body:
I was trying to ignore several patterns using the `PatternMatchingEventHandler` class. In the `dispatch` method, we have that behavior:

```python
...
if match_any_paths(paths, **kwargs):
    self.on_any_event(event)
   ...
```

It means that if I want to ignore `.tmp` files and if I rename a file from `test.txt` to `test.tmp`, then the event handler will still take into account that change. That is because we filter on all paths and if one is not ignored, then it considers the event as a one.

I think, if I understand correctly, the this is not the desired behavior.

On the other hand, if we check the `dispatch` method of `RegexMatchingEventHandler`:

```python
...
if any(r.match(p) for r in self.ignore_regexes for p in paths):
    return

if any(r.match(p) for r in self.regexes for p in paths):
    self.on_any_event(event)
   ...
```

Clearly, here, if one of the paths is ignored, then we stop now. That is the wanted behavior.

So, should I send a PR to fix `PatternMatchingEventHandler` behavior? Perhaps I misunderstand how to use that class or its purpose, let me know.

issue_title: Markov chain based response algorithm
issue_body:
ChatterBot is allows alternate statement selection algorithms to be passed into it's constructor. The default selection algorithm is `engram`, which looks for the closest existing match to a statement in the database and then returns a known response to that statement.

At the moment there is a placeholder file in `chatterbot/algorithms/markov.py` that is intended to be created as an option for selecting statements.

The markov algorithm would retrieve a list of statements that can be recognized as matches to the input text. A [Markov chain](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Markov_chain) based algorithm can then be used to build a new response based on the collection of matching statements.

- Ideally, a method to validate the grammatical correctness of the newly created statement could also be created. It may be useful to look into [NLTK ](http://www.nltk.org/) to determine this validation.
- I'm open to suggestions on how to determine the number of words required to satisfy the markov algorithm.

issue_title: Race condition makes restart/reconnect impossible in some cases
issue_body:
I'm finding that if I create a StompConnection12 object with reconnect_sleep_initial set to its default value of 0.1, then if I quickly stop and restart activemq while my listener is running, it will fail to properly connect. However, if I set reconnect_sleep_initial=10, the code generally reconnects successfully. 

I suspect there's some kind of race condition between maybe the wait_on_connection and receiver loop code, where wait_on_connection's thread condition it's waiting on is being awakened by the receiver loop's 'disconnect' notification from the previous disconnect, or maybe the receiver loop's setting of self.running to False from a previous disconnection or something, but I can't determine exactly which. 

My code is below. Any advice is welcome.

```
def start_consumer():
    connection = stomp.StompConnection12([(STOMP_HOST, STOMP_PORT)],
                                         heartbeats=(STOMP_HEARTBEAT_SEND_MIN_MS, STOMP_HEARTBEAT_RECV_MAX_MS),
                                         reconnect_attempts_max=CONNECT_RETRIES_MAX,
                                         reconnect_sleep_initial=10,
                                         timeout=10)
    listener = StompConsumer()
    connection.set_listener('StompConsumer', listener)
    connection.start()
    connection.connect(STOMP_USER, STOMP_PASS, wait=True)
    connection.subscribe(STOMP_QUEUE, id=1)
    return connection


if __name__ == '__main__':
    conn = start_consumer()
    while True:
        if conn.is_connected():
            time.sleep(2)
        else:
            conn = start_consumer()
```

issue_title: Serious bug in apply_bpe (mutable default parameter)
issue_body:
I think there is a serious bug in `apply_bpe.py` resulting from having a mutable default parameter (`cache={}`) in the `encode` function (https://github.com/rsennrich/subword-nmt/blob/master/apply_bpe.py#L126).

I guess it's fine if you use the script as a command-line tool, but if I use multiple instances of the `BPE` class directly, I observe the following behavior:
```
In [1]: from apply_bpe import BPE

In [2]: bpe_de = BPE(open("/path/to/german.codes"))

In [3]: bpe_en = BPE(open("/path/to/english.codes"))

In [4]: bpe_de.segment("bewundere")
Out[4]: 'bewunder@@ e'

In [5]: bpe_en.segment("bewundere")
Out[5]: 'bewunder@@ e'
```
During the first `segment` call the `cache` dictionary is modified during `encode`, and the same instance of `cache` is then used in the second call of `encode`, essentially overwriting the codes loaded from file.

If I make the following simple modification
```
def encode(..., cache=None):
    if cache is None:
        cache = {}
    ...
```
I get the expected behavior:
```
In [1]: from apply_bpe import BPE

In [2]: bpe_de = BPE(open("/path/to/german.codes"))

In [3]: bpe_en = BPE(open("/path/to/english.codes"))

In [4]: bpe_de.segment("bewundere")
Out[4]: 'bewunder@@ e'

In [5]: bpe_en.segment("bewundere")
Out[5]: 'be@@ w@@ un@@ d@@ ere'
```

See also http://effbot.org/zone/default-values.htm

issue_title: Unnumbered figures have numbers in pdf output
issue_body:
I'm declaring this as an issue but will not likely address it until there is demand for a fix.

There are three different labeling possibilities arising from the syntax [proposed](https://github.com/jgm/pandoc/issues/813#issuecomment-70423503) by @scaramouche1 in pandoc Issue #813.

 1) Numbered, referenceable figures:

        ![Caption.](fig.png){#fig:A}

 2) Numbered, unreferenceable figures:

        ![Caption.](fig.png){#fig:}

 3) Unnumbered, unreferenceable figures:

        ![Caption.](fig.png){}

Presently, pandoc-fignos does the right thing for html output.  For PDF output, however, the case 3 figures are numbered.  This is because pandoc's TeX writer only produces numbered figures.

Notes:

  * A possible workaround in pandoc-fignos: Disable automatic caption numbering in LaTeX altogether and hard-code it as is done for the other output formats.  No pretty, but it could be used in a pinch.

  * LaTeX allows unnumbered figures by using the `\caption*` command in the [caption](https://www.ctan.org/tex-archive/macros/latex/contrib/caption/) package.

issue_body:
* parglare version: 0.5
* Python version: 3.6.3
* Operating System: Mac OSX High Sierra

### Description

I am trying to implement loop behavior, specifically a while loop using custom actions with my grammar. When I run the parser, it executes the statements before evaluating the loop conditions and then continues runs the loop with no loop body.

### What I Did

I defined the grammar as below:

STATEMENT_LIST:         STATEMENT STATEMENT_LIST?;
STATEMENT:              CONCEPT_STATEMENT
                      | EXPRESSION_STATEMENT
                      | ACCEPT_STATEMENT
                      | ANSWER_STATEMENT
                      | WHILE_STATEMENT
                      | IF_STATEMENT
                      | IMPORT_STATEMENT;
EXPRESSION_STATEMENT:   EXPRESSION_STATEMENT AND_OP EXPRESSION_STATEMENT {right, 1}
                      | EXPRESSION_STATEMENT OR_OP EXPRESSION_STATEMENT  {right, 1}
                      | EXPRESSION_STATEMENT GE_OP EXPRESSION_STATEMENT  {right, 2}
                      | EXPRESSION_STATEMENT LE_OP EXPRESSION_STATEMENT  {right, 2}
                      | EXPRESSION_STATEMENT GT_OP EXPRESSION_STATEMENT  {right, 2}
                      | EXPRESSION_STATEMENT LT_OP EXPRESSION_STATEMENT  {right, 2}
                      | EXPRESSION_STATEMENT EQ_OP EXPRESSION_STATEMENT  {right, 2}
                      | EXPRESSION_STATEMENT NE_OP EXPRESSION_STATEMENT  {right, 3}
                      | NOT_OP EXPRESSION_STATEMENT
                      | L_PAREN_PN EXPRESSION_STATEMENT R_PAREN_PN
                      | TAUTOLOGY
                      | CONTRADICTION
                      | IDENTIFIER
                      | NUMBER;
WHILE_STATEMENT:        WHILE_KW EXPRESSION_STATEMENT STATEMENT_LIST;

And the actions below:
actions_dict["PROGRAM"] = lambda _, nodes: nodes[0]
actions_dict["STATEMENT"] = lambda _, nodes: nodes[0]
actions_dict["EXPRESSION_STATEMENT"] = [lambda _, nodes: nodes[0] and nodes[2],
                                            lambda _, nodes: nodes[0] or nodes[2],
                                            lambda _, nodes: nodes[0] >= nodes[2],
                                            lambda _, nodes: nodes[0] <= nodes[2],
                                            lambda _, nodes: nodes[0] > nodes[2],
                                            lambda _, nodes: nodes[0] < nodes[2],
                                            lambda _, nodes: nodes[0] == nodes[2],
                                            lambda _, nodes: nodes[0] != nodes[2],
                                            lambda _, nodes: not nodes[1],
                                            lambda _, nodes: nodes[1],
                                            lambda _, nodes: bool(nodes[0]),
                                            lambda _, nodes: bool(nodes[0]),
                                            lambda _, nodes: nodes[0],
                                            lambda _, nodes: float(nodes[0])]
def while_func(context, nodes):
    print(nodes)
    while(nodes[1]):
        nodes[0]
    return None
actions_dict["WHILE_STATEMENT"] = while_func
actions_dict["ACCEPT_STATEMENT"] = lambda _, nodes: print("the answer is 42")

This parses the statement "WHILE 1==1  accept.request" correctly but when executing, It will print the answer once and then begin to execute the while loop with no output. I have tried executing the statement with and without the build tree flag but it gives the same behavior. I am looking for any advice on how to go about this.

issue_title: Fix issue on Ackley Function
issue_body:
* PySwarms version: 0.1.5
* Python version: 2.7
* Operating System: Linux

### Description

It seems that the Ackley function is not working on Python 2.7 version

```python
def ackley_func(x):
    if not np.logical_and(x >= -32, x <= 32).all():
        raise ValueError('Input for Ackley function must be within [-32, 32].')

    d = x.shape[1]
    j = (-20.0 * np.exp(-0.2 * np.sqrt((1/d) * (x**2).sum(axis=1)))
        - np.exp((1/float(d)) * np.cos(2 * np.pi * x).sum(axis=1))
        + 20.0
        + np.exp(1))

    return j
```

When given an input `np.zeros([3,2])`, most Python 3.X versions return something that `isclose` to `np.zeros(3)`. But when ran against a Python 2.7.X version, we're getting `[1.718, 1.718, 1.718]`

issue_title: Stopwatch plugin doesn't work with unbound methods on Python 2.7
issue_body:
In a similar vein to #22, the Stopwatch plugin fails with an unbound method:

```python
    def wantMethod(self, method):
        """
        Do we want to run this method?  See _should_run.
        """
        fullname = '%s.%s.%s' % (method.__module__,
                                 method.__self__.__class__.__name__,
                                 method.__name__)
        return self._should_run(fullname)
```

But if the method is unbound, which it seems to be when this code is called, then `method.__self__` is `None` and so `method.__self__.__class__.__name__` is `NoneType`.

In the same way that #22 uses `method.im_class` if it exists, which it will for an unbound method, I think we should check and use that first:

```python
    def wantMethod(self, method):
        """
        Do we want to run this method?  See _should_run.
        """
        # Use the class that asked for the unbound method
        if hasattr(method, 'im_class'):
            fullname = '%s.%s.%s' % (method.__module__,
                                     method.im_class.__name__,
                                     method.__name__)
        else:
            fullname = '%s.%s.%s' % (method.__module__,
                                     method.__self__.__class__.__name__,
                                     method.__name__)
        return self._should_run(fullname)
```

issue_title: Do RetryExecutor and TimeoutExecutor work well together?
issue_body:
In the TimeoutExecutor implementation from 1.7.0, futures are cancelled on timeout.  Does that work properly with the RetryExecutor?

My expectation:

- If the *outer* future is cancelled, that should be passed through and RetryExecutor stops handling the future -> that probably works now, so a TimeoutExecutor wrapping a RetryExecutor probably behaves sensibly.
- If the *inner* future is cancelled, what does RetryExecutor do; does it retry?  I think the policy should be able to decide.  It should be a possibility to retry when the inner future is cancelled, so that a RetryExecutor wrapping a TimeoutExecutor can work (can retry on timeout).

issue_title: JSON file changes to YAML after OAuth request
Cache TIMEOUT should be higher than the largest period specified in rate
issue_body:
Django specifies a default timeout of [300 seconds](https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/1.8/ref/settings/#std:setting-CACHES-TIMEOUT). If there are ratelimit decorators that have a rate with a period that exceeds this number e.g. `5/h` (i.e. 5 requests per hour), would the key expire after Timeout & be eventually overwritten depending on the cache expiration policy ? I tried to re-create this scenario using `backends.locmem.LocMemCache` however after the expiry time, the key was still accessible. Perhaps its marked as stale and is not reliable. I'm not sure how other backends will behave. 

It would be nice if `django-ratelimit` can look at the `settings.CACHES` and alert if `TIMEOUT` is lesser than the largest period specified in rate.

issue_title: AngularJS plugin Smart Table issue when trying set default selected row
Any interest in a row highlight feature?
issue_body:
For a project I'm currently working on, I needed to implement a row highlighting feature. "How is this different from select" you might ask? While they sound similar, they have slightly different (and complementary) use cases:

1. Only a single row can be highlighted at a time. A good example of when this might be useful is in a master-detail view: the table holds a list of records and the currently highlighted record's details are displayed in a separate view.
2. When you need a combination of a master-detail view, as well as multi-selection to operate on the table's records. Eg. A list of records that can be selected individually or all at once with checkboxes, and that can be highlighted one at a time to see details about them.

With an additional directive (that I'm writing but can't release on Github because it's internal company code), one could also use highlight to implement keyboard navigation on the table (up/down to highlight rows)

The highlight feature sets a flag called `isHighlighted` on the model. It can also set the class `st-highlighted` (this directive is currently in my internal project but can be factored out into Smart-Table).

I have working code for this in my fork: https://github.com/jogjayr/Smart-Table and would be very happy to make an example Plunkr, write tests and documentation etc and make a nicely formatted pull request, if this feature is desirable in the main project. Please let me know.

issue_title: Facebook auth should include return_scopes to see which requested permissions have been granted
issue_body:
When the user approves FB login, they may grant all, some, or none of the scopes requested by the application. By passing `"return_scopes": true` as a parameter to the login URL, the data returned includes a list of scopes that were actually granted by the user.

I am happy to do the work to make this work, but I'm not entirely sure where this should go. It appears to be a parameter on the call to the `AUTHORIZATION_URL`, though when I add it to `SOCIAL_AUTH_FACEBOOK_EXTRA_ARGUMENTS` in settings it is not being picked up.

An alternate way to retrieve this info is calling the Facebook SDK (using facebook-sdk, for instance), but it seems this belongs in the authentication pipeline as that's where I am populating the database with the user's preferences and settings.

issue_title: Make it easier to disable social_details pipeline step
issue_body:
Sometimes you don't want to have any user fields automatically populated, and want to handle all of that explicitly, and the obvious way to achieve that is to disable the pipeline step that fills `details` with info from the social account.

The trouble is that currently the second default pipeline step expects a details argument that the previous pipeline step is supposed to supply.

How about feeding in a default empty details argument to the first pipeline step so that every pipeline step can expect it to be there?

issue_title: Misbehavior with async def methods
issue_body:
When decorating an async method the coroutine is cached instead of the value returned by the coroutine. This is certainly not the programmer's intent, and moreover pointless since coroutines can only be awaited once:
~~~~
$ ipython3
Python 3.6.4 (default, Feb  1 2018, 11:06:09) 
Type 'copyright', 'credits' or 'license' for more information
IPython 6.2.1 -- An enhanced Interactive Python. Type '?' for help.

In [1]: import asyncio
   ...: from cached_property import cached_property
   ...:     
   ...: class Foo(object):
   ...: 
   ...:     @cached_property
   ...:     async def bar(self):
   ...:         return 'value'
   ...: 
   ...:     async def baz(self):
   ...:         await self.bar
   ...:         await self.bar
   ...: 
   ...: 
   ...: import asyncio
   ...: asyncio.get_event_loop().run_until_complete(Foo().baz())          
   ...: 
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
RuntimeError                              Traceback (most recent call last)
<ipython-input-1-a740a5aac079> in <module>()
     14 
     15 import asyncio
---> 16 asyncio.get_event_loop().run_until_complete(Foo().baz())

/usr/lib64/python3.6/asyncio/base_events.py in run_until_complete(self, future)
    465             raise RuntimeError('Event loop stopped before Future completed.')
    466 
--> 467         return future.result()
    468 
    469     def stop(self):

<ipython-input-1-a740a5aac079> in baz(self)
     10     async def baz(self):
     11         await self.bar
---> 12         await self.bar
     13 
     14 

RuntimeError: cannot reuse already awaited coroutine
~~~~
When caching an async method, the value should be stored and repeated access should always return a new coroutine (which returns the cached value).

This may be related to #7, though its not quite clear to me what that ticket is about

issue_title: Consider having pyramid_swagger handle basic primitive conversion in query/path/bodies
issue_body:
One major source of repetitive busywork in Pyramid views is casting query and path parameters into their proper type in your view (e.g. /user/1 will appear in Pyramid's matchdict as a string, not an integer). A service's Swagger spec has the necessary context to do this at the tween level, and in fact we already have the mapping available for doing our own validation. For query bodies, we should just be strongly recommending JSON everywhere as it will handle its own typecasting.

This would simplify boilerplate in views which use these parameters, which often looks like:

``` python
@view_config('user_search')  # Serves /user/{id}/items?count=5&start=0
def user_search_view(request):
    id = int(request.matchdict['id'])
    start = int(request.params['start'])
    count = int(request.params['count'])
    user_item_search(id, count, start)
```

Would offering casting for incoming query and path parameters be of interest? The proposal would be to only support the types defined by Swagger, with no plans to implement richer types (that is up to service authors to do separately). As always, we can make the feature controlled by a setting in your Pyramid app's config.

issue_title: Handle unicode keys via ascii escaping

issue_title: fixed char fields not evolving correctly
PasswordType + Argon2 make VARBINARY(4294967331)
issue_body:
Passlib 1.7.0 support argon2 type hashing. I wanted to use it as Password hasing algorithm.

But sqlalchemy-utils generate `VARBINARY(4294967331)` field. It is too huge!

Size of PasswordType is depend on `max_salt_size` value and [Argon2's `max_salt_size` in passlib impl](https://bitbucket.org/ecollins/passlib/src/9572e99efc8ab5e75dea62cf474889c9ed00ac60/passlib/handlers/argon2.py?at=default&fileviewer=file-view-default#argon2.py-123) is `MAX_UINT32`.

I think you write some notice on docs or make some arguments to PasswordType for limit salt size. Default salt size setting is `16` ([argon2_cffi(passlib recommand it)](https://github.com/hynek/argon2_cffi/blob/master/src/argon2/_password_hasher.py#L13), [passlib](https://bitbucket.org/ecollins/passlib/src/9572e99efc8ab5e75dea62cf474889c9ed00ac60/passlib/handlers/argon2.py?at=default&fileviewer=file-view-default#argon2.py-66))

I think the part of my scenario that confuses `win-unicode-console` is that I'm redirecting a subprocess' stdout to `os.devnull`. This is key to the problem, because if I redirect it to a temp file instead, the problem goes away. I guess `os.devnull` does not mimic a real file in some respect.

Attached are `hello_world.py`, `fails.py`, and `succeeds.py`. The last two scripts invoke `hello_world` which just prints to the console. The only difference between `fails` and `succeeds` is that `fails` pipes its subprocess' stdout to `os.devnull`, while `succeeds` uses a temp file.

The attachments have a `.txt` file extension to keep github happy. Remove that before running the files.

issue_title: Validate lookup (and provide helpful error)
issue_body:
It would be helpful if the `NestedMixin` did a quality check on the lookup keyword (i.e. that all characters are valid for a regex group name) and provided a descriptive error message if they are not.  For example, including a hyphen in this value results in an error like:

```
django.core.exceptions.ImproperlyConfigured: "^parents/(?P<ui-parent_pk>[^/.]+)/childs/$" is not a valid regular expression: bad character in group name u'ui-parent_pk'
```

I've customized routers before so it was obvious to me what went wrong, but I doubt its obvious to first-time users of the package.  The issue came up because I normally use a pattern like:

```
api.register(parents', ParentViewSet, base_name='parent')
nested_router = NestedSingletonRouter(api, parents', lookup='parent')
nested_router.register(child', ParentChildViewSet, base_name='parent-child')
```

I needed to expose less permissive serializers for my UI so I ran into the error making "ui" versions.

```
api.register(parents', ParentViewSet, base_name='ui-parent')
nested_router = NestedSingletonRouter(api, parents', lookup='ui-parent')  # cannot use a hyphen in lookup
nested_router.register(child', ParentChildViewSet, base_name='ui-parent-child')
```

This may not be a huge issue, but it seems like an straightforward mistake to make and an easy one to catch/correct.  If this sounds reasonable, I should be able to get around to a PR.

issue_title: Default ActivationView doesn't check whether user is already activated
issue_body:
In the [default ActivationView](https://github.com/macropin/django-registration/blob/v1.4/registration/backends/default/views.py#L119-L138), the call to `RegistrationProfile.activate_user` returns the `User` object when the user is first activated but also if the user is already activated. So in the view, there's no way for it to know whether it's the initial activation. 

```python
class ActivationView(BaseActivationView):
    def activate(self, *args, **kwargs):
        activation_key = kwargs.get('activation_key', '')
        activated_user = (RegistrationProfile.objects
                          .activate_user(activation_key))
        if activated_user:
            signals.user_activated.send(sender=self.__class__,
                                        user=activated_user,
                                        request=self.request)
        return activated_user
```

This leads to two issues:
* The `user_activated` signal is called each time a user clicks an activation link. Say for example, if this signal is used to send a notification, that notification will get sent every time.
* If `REGISTRATION_AUTO_LOGIN` is set to `True`, the activation link can be used as a login link indefinitely. 

Note: [this is an issue in the original `django-registration` as well](https://github.com/ubernostrum/django-registration/blob/2.2/registration/views.py#L93-L113)

issue_title: Smiley Codes Can Pre-Empt Each Other.
issue_body:
## Expected Behavior
If two custom smilies are set that where one has a code that contains a subset of the other code, each code should produce the associated smiley.

## Actual Behavior
The code for the longer smiley will be pre-empted by the code for the smaller smiley, leaving the smaller smiley followed by the rest of the code.

## Steps to Reproduce the Problem

  1. Create a smiley for the standard code ':p'
  2. Create a smiley for the standard forum smiley ':ph34r:'
  3. Render text that contains ':ph34r:'

The rendered text will display "😛h34r:" instead of the expected ninja smiley.

## Specifications

  - Version: master
  - Platform: Ubuntu 16
  - Subsystem: Django 2.0.4
