issue_title: _mysql_exceptions.Warning: Specified key was too long; max key length is 767 bytes
issue_body:
During the migration 0001_initial the mysql engine reports the following error/warning:
_mysql_exceptions.Warning: Specified key was too long; max key length is 767 bytes

This is caused by the tree_path field of max_length=500 and a db_index which apparently exceeds the hard limit of 767. 
tree_path = models.CharField(_('Tree path'), max_length=500, editable=False, db_index=True)

The migration fails to complete yet the table and an index on tree_path are created. MySQL will truncate the index keys.

issue_title: Remove admin.autodiscover() for URLconf example
issue_body:
I suggest to replace:

```
from ratelimitbackend import admin

admin.autodiscover()

urlpatterns += patterns('',
    (r'^admin/', include(admin.site.urls)),
)
```

with:

```
from ratelimitbackend import admin

urlpatterns += [
    (r'^admin/', include(admin.site.urls)),
]
```

which is the recommended style as of Django 1.7, the oldest currently
supported Django version.

I_PORT => w_port,

issue_title: Add rules for asserts
Support custom header and data fonts
Update documentation with font hinting status
issue_body:
In the Material Design specification there is a note indicating that [hinted fonts](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Font_hinting) should be used on Chrome OS, Windows, and Linux. Since hinting can affect the way fonts are rendered in the browser, this prompted me to look at this package to verify that it did have hinting. To verify this I ran a few of the .woff files through [fonttools](https://github.com/behdad/fonttools) and did see that at least a handful of the files did including hinting.

It might be nice to include a note in this package's documentation that the included fonts do include hinting.

issue_title: Prevent detection of imports in comments (SCSS)
issue_body:
Bootstrap 4 currently contains an import in a comment (https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap/blob/1a6d0cc/scss/_reboot.scss#L47) that would create an infinite loop if it were outside a comment. Since the SCSS dependency detection ignores comments, it detects the import even though it shouldn't and thus creates the infinite loop: bootstrap.scss -> _reboot.scss -> bootstrap.scss -> …

The SCSS compiler needs to handle comments and ignore everything in there to prevent this bug in the future.

issue_title: Exit status is set to 0 on failure instead of 1
issue_body:
When running tests with nose and the flaky plugin, the exit status was not being set to 1 when the only tests that failed were ones that run with the flaky decorator and had max tries greater than 1.  If a test that was not decorated with `@flaky` also failed, then the exit status was properly set to 1.  

For example, here is a test that always fails, run with nose and without flaky.  The exit status is `1`.
```
$ nosetests test_flaky.py:TestFlaky
F
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
1) FAIL: test_flaky_exit (test_flaky.TestFlaky)

   Traceback (most recent call last):
    test_flaky.py line 7 in test_flaky_exit
      self.assertTrue(False)
   AssertionError: False is not true


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
1 test run in 0.0 seconds. 
1 FAILED (0 tests passed)
$ echo $?
1
```

... And here is the same test, run with nose and using flaky with the default 2 max tries.  It correctly runs the test twice and prints the correct results, but then exits with `0` status.
```
$ nosetests test_flaky.py:TestFlaky --with-flaky
-F
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
1) FAIL: test_flaky_exit (test_flaky.TestFlaky)

   Traceback (most recent call last):
    test_flaky.py line 7 in test_flaky_exit
      self.assertTrue(False)
   AssertionError: False is not true


-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
2 tests run in 0.0 seconds. 
1 FAILED, 1 skipped (0 tests passed)
$ echo $?
0
```

The root cause is that the flaky plugin passes a newly created `TextTestResult` object to `_rerun_test` rather than the original object that nose created, leaving nose unaware of any rerun results.

issue_title: Create auth test suite that is run for all auth subclasses
issue_body:
#197 was able to occur because we didn't test that `JWTAuth.refresh()` followed the same rules as `OAuth2.refresh()`. There is an `OAuth2` test case for the scenario that triggered #197, but not a `JWTAuth` test case for the same scenario.

We should have a common test suite that can be used to test the common interface of all auth subclass. And then make sure we run that suite for all of the subclasses defined in this project.

issue_title: Link migration to git tag or commit
issue_body:
If we check out latest commit and play all migrations it seems like it not chronologically correct migrations because in different time code-slice looks differently.

Example:
```
     v1     v2     v3
-----*------*------*-----> 
     t1     t2     t3    time
```
In `t1` we create migration `v1` and code contain lines
```
...
name = field.Char()
...
```
To the moment `t2` we add more code and create migration `v2`
```
...
name = field.Char()
age = field.Integer()
...
```
To the moment `t3` we introduce new changes and create migration `v3`
```
...
person_id = field.Many2one(...)
...
```
If we need to run all migration on the new server we check out `v3` code and on `v1`  we already install code that should be migrated late. and in the moment of `v2` migration, we receiving an error because the code hasn't appropriate fields.

issue_title: Model inference outputs not matching across MXNet versions using save_params and load_params
issue_body:
## Description
Model inference outputs not matching when model is saved on MXNet v1.0.0 and the same model is loaded on MXNet v1.3.0 (built from latest master branch). 
The error was observed in the CI run for Model Backwards Compatibility Check and is available here  : http://jenkins.mxnet-ci.amazon-ml.com/blue/organizations/jenkins/restricted-backwards-compatibility-checker/detail/restricted-backwards-compatibility-checker/1/pipeline/ 

## Error Message:
Items are not equal:
```Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "model_backwards_compat_inference.py", line 135, in <module>
    test_lenet_gluon_load_params_api()
  File "model_backwards_compat_inference.py", line 72, in test_lenet_gluon_load_params_api
    assert_almost_equal(old_inference_results.asnumpy(), output.asnumpy())
  File "/work/mxnet/python/mxnet/test_utils.py", line 493, in assert_almost_equal
    raise AssertionError(msg)
AssertionError: 
Items are not equal:
Error 1.969787 exceeds tolerance rtol=0.000010, atol=0.000000.  Location of maximum error:(5, 0), a=0.003014, b=0.003014
 a: array([[ 0.01743407, -0.30903903],
       [ 0.08352755, -0.365019  ],
       [ 0.06324662, -0.4323489 ],...
 b: array([[ 0.01743408, -0.3090391 ],
       [ 0.08352771, -0.365019  ],
       [ 0.06324662, -0.4323488 ],...
```

## Minimum reproducible example
Perform the training and save the model on MXNet 1.0.0.
Model Definition:
```
class Net(gluon.Block):
    def __init__(self, **kwargs):
        super(Net, self).__init__(**kwargs)
        with self.name_scope():
            # layers created in name_scope will inherit name space
            # from parent layer.
            self.conv1 = nn.Conv2D(20, kernel_size=(5, 5))
            self.pool1 = nn.MaxPool2D(pool_size=(2, 2), strides=(2, 2))
            self.conv2 = nn.Conv2D(50, kernel_size=(5, 5))
            self.pool2 = nn.MaxPool2D(pool_size=(2, 2), strides=(2, 2))
            self.fc1 = nn.Dense(500)
            self.fc2 = nn.Dense(2)

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.pool1(F.tanh(self.conv1(x)))
        x = self.pool2(F.tanh(self.conv2(x)))
        # 0 means copy over size from corresponding dimension.
        # -1 means infer size from the rest of dimensions.
        x = x.reshape((0, -1))
        x = F.tanh(self.fc1(x))
        x = F.tanh(self.fc2(x))
        return x

```

Training code snippet : 
```
def train_lenet_gluon_save_params_api():
    model_name = 'lenet_gluon_save_params_api'
    create_model_folder(model_name)
    logging.info('Saving files for model %s' % model_name)
    net = Net()
    weights = mx.initializer.Xavier(magnitude=2.57)
    net.initialize(weights, ctx=[mx.cpu(0)])
    # Prepare data

    test_data = mx.nd.array(np.random.uniform(-1, 1, size=(20, 1, 30, 30)))
    output = net(test_data)
    # print (y)

    mx.nd.save(os.path.join(get_model_path(model_name), ''.join([model_name, '-data'])), {'data': test_data})
    save_inference_results(output, model_name)
    net.save_params(os.path.join(get_model_path(model_name), ''.join([model_name, '-params'])))
```

Model Inference to be performed on MXNet built from source from the latest [master](https://github.com/apache/incubator-mxnet)
Inference snippet : 
```
def test_lenet_gluon_load_params_api():
    from mxnet.test_utils import assert_almost_equal
    model_name = 'lenet_gluon_save_params_api'
    logging.info('Performing inference for model/API %s' % model_name)

    data = mx.nd.load(''.join([model_name, '-data']))
    test_data = data['data']
    # Load the model and perform inference
    loaded_model = Net()
    loaded_model.load_params(model_name + '-params')
    output = loaded_model(test_data)
    old_inference_results = mx.nd.load(model_name + '-inference')['inference']
    assert_almost_equal(old_inference_results.asnumpy(), output.asnumpy())
    logging.info('=================================')
    logging.info('Assertion passed for model : %s' % model_name)

```
## Steps to reproduce

1. Run the training on MXNet 1.0.0 ( Installed via pip install mxnet==1.0.0)
2. Run the inference on MXNet 1.3.0 ( Built from source using latest master branch)

This regression is not seen on models trained on v1.1.0 and v1.2.0 with inference performed on v1.3.0

issue_title: MKLDNN fails in the backward computation when forward runs with is_train=False
issue_body:
This is a pretty special case. When we run forward with is_train=False and MKLDNN is enabled, backward fails with a memory error. @ashokei @pengzhao-intel @TaoLv 

```python
def test_hybrid_static_memory():
    x = mx.nd.random.uniform(shape=(2, 3, 32, 32))
    x.attach_grad()

    net1 = gluon.model_zoo.vision.get_resnet(
        1, 18, pretrained=True, prefix='net_', ctx=mx.context.current_context())
    net2 = gluon.model_zoo.vision.get_resnet(
        1, 18, pretrained=True, prefix='net_', ctx=mx.context.current_context())
    net1(x)
    net2(x)

    net1.save_params('test.params')
    net2.load_params('test.params')

    def test(net, x):
        with mx.autograd.record(False):
            y = net(x) + net(x)
            y.backward()

        grads = {k: v.grad() for k, v in net.collect_params().items() if v.grad_req != 'null'}

        return y, grads

    y1, grads1 = test(net1, x)
    y2, grads2 = test(net2, x)

    assert_almost_equal(y1.asnumpy(), y2.asnumpy(), rtol=1e-3, atol=1e-5)
    for key in grads1:
        print(key)
        try:
            assert_almost_equal(grads1[key].asnumpy(), grads2[key].asnumpy(), rtol=1e-3, atol=1e-5)
        except Exception as e:
            print(e)
```

For bugs or installation issues, please provide the following information.
The more information you provide, the more likely people will be able to help you.

## Error Message:
===================================================================

**I want to know what does the mx.contrib.sym.ctc_loss(data, label) return ?**

Signature: mx.contrib.sym.ctc_loss(*args, **kwargs)
Docstring:
Connectionist Temporal Classification Loss.

The shapes of the inputs and outputs:

- **data**: *(sequence_length, batch_size, alphabet_size + 1)*
- **label**: *(batch_size, label_sequence_length)*
- **out**: *(batch_size)*.

``label`` is a tensor of integers between 1 and *alphabet_size*. If a 
sequence of labels is shorter than *label_sequence_length*, use the special 
padding character 0 at the end of the sequence to conform it to the correct 
length. For example, if *label_sequence_length* = 4, and one has two sequences 
of labels [2, 1] and [3, 2, 2], the resulting ```label``` tensor should be 
padded to be::

  [[2, 1, 0, 0], [3, 2, 2, 0]]

The ``data`` tensor consists of sequences of activation vectors. The layer 
applies a softmax to each vector, which then becomes a vector of probabilities 
over the alphabet. Note that the 0th element of this vector is reserved for the 
special blank character.

See *Connectionist Temporal Classification: Labelling Unsegmented 
Sequence Data with Recurrent Neural Networks*, A. Graves *et al*. for more 
information.



Defined in src/operator/contrib/ctc_loss.cc:L79

Parameters
----------
data : Symbol
    Input data to the ctc_loss op.
label : Symbol
    Ground-truth labels for the loss.

name : string, optional.
    Name of the resulting symbol.

Returns
-------
Symbol
    The result symbol.

===================================================================
my network defined below:

def sym_ocrCTC(batch_size=100, num_layers=2, num_hidden=128, seq_len=30):
    stack = mx.rnn.SequentialRNNCell()
    for i in range(num_layers):
        if i==(num_layers-1): # laster layer
            stack.add(mx.rnn.LSTMCell(num_hidden=63, prefix='lstm_l%d_'%i))
        else:
            stack.add(mx.rnn.LSTMCell(num_hidden=num_hidden, prefix='lstm_l%d_'%i))

    data = mx.sym.Variable('data') # has the shape of (Batch_size, 30, 100)
    label = mx.sym.Variable('label')

    stack.reset()
    outputs, states = stack.unroll(seq_len, inputs=data, merge_outputs=True)
    
    pred = mx.sym.swapaxes(data=outputs, dim1=0, dim2=1)
    loss = mx.contrib.sym.ctc_loss(data=pred, label=label)
    ctc_loss = mx.sym.make_loss(loss)
    return ctc_loss

Is there any problem ? @piiswrong

issue_title: MxNet can't fully utilize CPU in multithread programs
issue_body:
Hi, all:
I'm using mxnet scala package and runs prediction in a multithread program and each thread has a thread local FeedForward instance. It seems that mxnet is not able to utilize multi cores since only one core is busy while I have 20 threads in this program.  The java stacktrace show that all  threads are in   this mxNDArraySyncCopyToCPU method. Is this method waiting some lock for all threads in the same process? One quick solution may be to  use multi process instead of multi thread but jvm has much heavy overhead and this is may be my last resort. Is there any way to solve this issue?

issue_title: CI times out during GIT checkout

issue_title: Exit race condition on library handle usage
issue_body:
There seems to be some sort of race condition on the _LIB handle (in base.py) when calling exit() from python.

Code:
```
import mxnet as mx
data = mx.sym.Variable('data')
# del data
exit(0)
```
The code above occasionally trigger the following exception:
```
Exception ignored in: <bound method SymbolBase.__del__ of <Symbol data>>
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/OSM/CBR/D61_RCV/work/and502/build/mxnet/python/mxnet/_ctypes/symbol.py", line 46, in __del__
AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'NNSymbolFree'
```

**NOTE:** the error is only triggered "sometimes", and the particular missing attribute may be other than 'NNSymbolFree'. 

I guess the issue is that the _LIB handle gets deleted and set to None before all the objects using it have been deleted. If uncommenting the "del data" line, the problem seems to go away, or is at least a lot harder to trigger.

This happens in master as of today using python 3 under Ubuntu 16.04.

issue_title: Compose Custom Loss with Softmax
issue_body:
Hello!

I'm trying to create a custom loss function using symbolic variables. The loss function is the sum of the softmax error and other values computed from internal layers. I know I cannot achieve this with `SoftmaxOutput` so I am using `SoftmaxActivation` as my activation function after the final fc layer and `softmax_cross_entropy` as the function to compute softmax loss stacked with `MakeLoss`. However, I am unable to run this pipeline without getting an error:

`Check failed: (this->shape_.Size()) == (shape.Size()) TBlob.get_with_shape: new and old shape do not match total elements`

I know there is a shape mismatch but I'm not sure what the issue is

Here is some sample code showing the basic structure I want to achieve with the network:

```
data = mx.symbol.Variable(name='data')
fc1 = mx.symbol.FullyConnected(data=data, num_hidden=num_hidden, name='fc1')
act = mx.symbol.Activation(data=fc1, act_type='sigmoid')
fc2 = mx.symbol.FullyConnected(data=act, num_hidden=2, name='fc2')
label = mx.symbol.Variable(name='softmax_label')
softmax_activation = mx.symbol.SoftmaxActivation(data=fc2)
loss_1 = mx.symbol.softmax_cross_entropy(lhs=softmax_activation, rhs=label)
loss_2 = mx.symbol.sum(ms.symbol.square(fc1 - 0.5))
return mx.symbol.MakeLoss(data=loss_1+loss_2, name='makeloss')
```

Does this make sense? I'm pretty sure this is possible, but I'm not sure where I went wrong.

On its own, the following does not compile:

```
data = mx.symbol.Variable(name='data')
fc1 = mx.symbol.FullyConnected(data=act, num_hidden=2, name='fc1')
label = mx.symbol.Variable(name='softmax_label')
softmax_activation = mx.symbol.SoftmaxActivation(data=fc1)
loss_1 = mx.symbol.softmax_cross_entropy(lhs=softmax_activation, rhs=label)
return mx.symbol.MakeLoss(data=loss_1, name='makeloss')
```

while this does:

```
data = mx.symbol.Variable(name='data')
fc1 = mx.symbol.FullyConnected(data=act, num_hidden=2, name='fc1')
label = mx.symbol.Variable(name='softmax_label')
return mx.symbol.SoftmaxOutput(data=fc1, label=label)
```

issue_title: Add support for zlib decompression
issue_body:
bravado-core should be able to handle compressed responses.
These can be detected by the presence of a `Content-Encoding` header.
Specifically, I'm interested in support for decoding responses compressed by zlib, which have a `Content-Encoding: deflate` header. This would allow for decreased network latency and bandwidth for users if they want to use compression for their responses.

issue_title: keyczar and pycrypto
issue_body:
`db/fields/encrypted.py` has a dependency on keyczar, which has a dependency on pycrypto, which has long since been deprecated and replaced. However, neither seem to be alive. There's an open 6 month old [issue](https://github.com/google/keyczar/issues/208) on keyczar to replace pycrypto with no feedback. 

Are there any alternatives? Are there any plans to update these dependencies?

issue_title: Detect invalid falsy event handlers
issue_body:
As of jQuery 3.0, event handlers that are falsy but not `false` now throw an error at some point in their lifecycle; in the past if the argument was falsy it would be a chainable no-op. Check for this case, warn about it, and return it to being a chainable no-op. Might as well also warn for crazy things like strings or numbers there, it really needs to be a function.

Ref https://github.com/jquery/jquery/issues/2812 (reverted for 1.12.0/2.2.0), https://github.com/jquery/jquery/pull/2249, https://github.com/jquery/jquery/pull/2248

issue_title: Error trying to delete a reference with an empty source during upgrade to Plone 5
issue_body:
I'm running an upgrade from 4.0.10 to 5.0, via an intermediate 4.3.7 step.  While on 4.3.7 I migrate all content to plone.app.contenttypes before starting the 5.0 upgrade.

On 5.0, the upgrade fails with the following traceback:

```
2015-12-07 22:40:56 ERROR plone.app.upgrade Upgrade aborted. Error:
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/Users/fulvio/Downloads/Plone-5.0/buildout-cache/eggs/Products.CMFPlone-5.0-py2.7.egg/Products/CMFPlone/MigrationTool.py", line 257, in upgrade
    step['step'].doStep(setup)
  File "/Users/fulvio/Downloads/Plone-5.0/buildout-cache/eggs/Products.GenericSetup-1.8.0-py2.7.egg/Products/GenericSetup/upgrade.py", line 166, in doStep
    self.handler(tool)
  File "/Users/fulvio/Downloads/Plone-5.0/buildout-cache/eggs/plone.app.upgrade-1.3.18-py2.7.egg/plone/app/upgrade/v50/betas.py", line 353, in to50rc1
    migrate_linkintegrity_relations(portal)
  File "/Users/fulvio/Downloads/Plone-5.0/buildout-cache/eggs/plone.app.linkintegrity-3.0.2-py2.7.egg/plone/app/linkintegrity/upgrades.py", line 21, in migrate_linkintegrity_relations
    source_obj, target_obj, relationship=referencedRelationship)
  File "/Users/fulvio/Downloads/Plone-5.0/buildout-cache/eggs/Products.Archetypes-1.10.10-py2.7.egg/Products/Archetypes/ReferenceEngine.py", line 343, in deleteReference
    sID, sobj = self._uidFor(source)
  File "/Users/fulvio/Downloads/Plone-5.0/buildout-cache/eggs/Products.Archetypes-1.10.10-py2.7.egg/Products/Archetypes/ReferenceEngine.py", line 533, in _uidFor
    raise ReferenceException, "%r not referenceable" % uobject
ReferenceException: None not referenceable
```

In the `plone.app.linkintegrity.upgrades.migrate_linkintegrity_relations` call, `brain` has two non-empty values for `sourceUID` and `targetUID`, but `uuidToObject(brain.sourceUID)` produces `None`, while `uuidToObject(brain.targetUID)` is a jpg image object.
After that, `reference_catalog.deleteReference` is called with a `None` `source_obj`, and from there things go downhill.

I don't know why `brain.sourceUID` does not resolve to a valid object, and I don't know what should be done about it, but it seems that somewhere along the line there should be a safeguard against this case.

Also, if anyone has any suggestions on how to get around this so I can finish my upgrade, I would be much obliged.

issue_title: clean escapes tags found in a <script> even if they're allowed
issue_body: In bleach 1.5.0 clean would only filter not allowed tags: ``` >>> from bleach import clean >>> clean('', ['b']) '<script>Hello</script>' ``` bleach 2.0 doesn't honor the allowed tag when there are other tags: ``` >>> clean('', ['b']) '<script><b>Hello</b></script>' >>> # But: >>> clean('Hello', ['b']) 'Hello' ```

issue_title: Rework clients CLI flags
issue_body:
Let's use non positionnal arguments for all our current CLI tools.

Python and NodeJS already depends on argparse, so we can easily just add `--` to them.

Only C++ would require more love, but we can likely find a library compatible with argparse, so we get consistent accross all and avoid the nasty effects we had to tackle.

issue_title: X3D support
issue_body:
The [wishlist](http://www.assimp.org/wishlist.txt) lists VRML as a format that may get support in the future.

VRML has been superseded by [X3D](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/X3D), so instead of (maybe) adding support for VRML, X3D support should probably be added instead.

X3D is "widely" used for 3D on websites (widely in quotes, because 3D on the web is still pretty rare).

X3D is just XML, so it should be pretty easy to parse. It's also an open format, so specifications should be readily available.

I'm creating this issue mostly just so I can see if/when this gets implemented. So just another wishlist item. :smile:

issue_title: Crash when freeing the progresshandler
issue_body:
I get crashes in my application, because the pointer to progress handler i pass is a pointer to the class in which i create the Importer object (the object that manages the progress of my task and has acces to my GUI's progress bar). So when my import method goes out of scope, Importer is being destroyed, and tries to destroy the object i am currently in when deleting the progress handler.
Even when not in this case, there could still be issues if the ProgressHandler object was not allocated with the same runtime library as the library.

I worked around it by setting the progresshandler to NULL just before destruction, but it's not something i should have to do.

The library seems to have it's own custom heap. Couldn't it check if the pointer is owned by this heap, and otherwise not free it or something like that?

issue_title: Bugs with jsonschema types
issue_body:
* If a definition's type is an array of types (e.g. ['null', 'integer']) it will blow up.
* If an array definition doesn't define an example, an error will be raised, e.g. `doctor.errors.TypeSystemError: creatives missing example. 'example'`.  In the previous version of doctor we would resolve the example based on the `items`.  We should probably do the same here.

issue_title: Make the IMTs extensible
issue_body:
Recently there were two request to introduce new IMTs in hazardlib:

1. new IMTs for geotechnical hazard
2. new IMTs for more sophisticated correlation models

Introducing a new IMTs is now simpler than in the past, but still not ideal. See for instance
https://github.com/gem/oq-engine/pull/3885; the user has to

1. modify the imt.py module
2. modify the oqvalidation.py module
3. modify the check_gsim.py module

Adding a new IMT requires forking the engine or opening a pull request to the main engine and wait months until it is reviewed. This is bad. We must make it possible *to add a new IMT without touching the engine code base at all*. While doing that one could take the occasion to fix early design errors.
In particular I have always regarded the fact that there is a new class for each IMT as a design error.
All IMTs should have the same class, they can by distinguished by their name (i.e. "PGA", "SA", etc)
and we just need a registry `IMT name -> factory function generating instances of that IMT`.
Then the user can add a new IMT in his own code. In the case of the IMTs introduced by Pablo he would need to write something like this

```python
from openquake.hazardlib import imt

imt.registry.add(
"FIV3", "Filtered Incremental Velocity (3)", "period")

imt.registry.add(
"SAAVG", "Average Spectral Acceleration", "period, damping", defaults=(5.,))

imt.registry.add(
"SDI", "Spectral inelastic displacement", "period, Cy, damping", defaults=(5.,))
```

and nothing else.

PS: an alternative, more backward compatible, would be to keep the IMTs as classes. Then Pablo
should write

```python
from openquake.hazardlib.imt import IMT, positivefloat

class FIV3(IMT):
    "Filtered Incremental Velocity (3)"
    _fields = [(period, positivefloat)]

class SAAVG(IMT):
     "Average Spectral Acceleration"
    _fields =  [("period", positivefloat), ("damping", positivefloat)]
    _defaults=(5.,)

class SDI(IMT):
    "Spectral inelastic displacement"
   _fields =  [("period", positivefloat),  ("Cy",positivefloat), ("damping", positivefloat)]
    _defaults=(5.,)
```

issue_title: Add spectral period range as attribute of GMPE (or define consistent attribute name for coefficients table)
issue_body:
GMPEs cannot be used outside of the define spectral period range. At present the user is notified of this in the hazard calculation as the CoeffsTable object will throw a key error should they attempt to run a calculation for a spectral period outside of the range defined. It would be useful, however, to know what the spectral period range of the GMPE is without having to call the get_mean_and_stddevs(). This is a problem for the GMPE toolkit, which tries to define the period range of a GMPE without calling it. It does this by reading the period limits from the coefficients table. However, some GMPEs (particularly the older ones) do not consistently use COEFFS to refer to the coefficients table, and in fact no consistent approach to defining the coefficients tables was adopted. This caused a problem in a recent PR (https://github.com/GEMScienceTools/gmpe-smtk/pull/57).

I suggest adopting one or both of the following:

1. Add an attribute to the GMPE class, e.g.:  `DEFINED_FOR_PERIOD_RANGE = (0.01, 10.0)`

2. Use the common term COEFFS for the "main" coefficients table of all GMPEs, such that the period range could be retrieved easily.

issue_title: Sporadic failures in `Popen()` under Windows with processes left in `subprocess._active` list
issue_body:
Under Windows, when running test-cases creating different temporary repositories consecutively, I get sporadic failures when executing the `git` process:

```
 File "D:\Apps\WinPython-64bit-3.5.2.1\python-3.5.2.amd64\lib\site-packages\git\repo\base.py", line 872, in init
    git.init(**kwargs)
  File "D:\Apps\WinPython-64bit-3.5.2.1\python-3.5.2.amd64\lib\site-packages\git\cmd.py", line 466, in <lambda>
    return lambda *args, **kwargs: self._call_process(name, *args, **kwargs)
  File "D:\Apps\WinPython-64bit-3.5.2.1\python-3.5.2.amd64\lib\site-packages\git\cmd.py", line 910, in _call_process
    return self.execute(make_call(), **_kwargs)
  File "D:\Apps\WinPython-64bit-3.5.2.1\python-3.5.2.amd64\lib\site-packages\git\cmd.py", line 630, in execute
    raise GitCommandNotFound(str(err))
git.exc.GitCommandNotFound: [WinError 6] The handle is invalid
```

- Digging a bit further, I noticed that before executing the failing `Popen()`, the `subprocess._active` list contains 2 leftovers processes, while this list is always empty when not failing.
- I tried to add some delays with `time.sleep()` but it did not help.
- It did help when I simplified my test-cases, i.e. dropping some commit actions.
- Tested under:
  - Windows 10, 
  - [WinPython 3.5.2](http://winpython.github.io/):
  - gitpython: both latest in PyPi `v2.0.8` and latest master from github(df65f51de) (`2.0.9-dev0`).
  - smmap: `0.9.0`
  - gitdb: `0.6.4`

An minor note: the try-catch `Popen()` code assumes that any exception caught is because `git` was not found, while above it obviously not the case.

issue_title: Validation on the instance itself when an instance method is called.
issue_body:
How to do this (hackily) using doubles and `with_args_validator` right now. Downsides: feels really hacky, not documented, only works if you have a reference to the exact object.
```python
    def test_matcher_with_no_args_and_inspect_instance(self):
        subject = InstanceDouble('doubles.testing.User')
        subject.name = 'Some Random Value'

        def tester(positional_arg):
            return positional_arg == 'Drew Carey' and subject.name == 'Bob Barker'

        expect(subject).method_with_positional_arguments.with_args_validator(tester)
        subject.name = 'Bob Barker'
        subject.method_with_positional_arguments('Drew Carey')
```

How to accomplish the same goal (much nicer & cleaner) using Mock: (Documentation: http://www.voidspace.org.uk/python/mock/examples.html#mocking-unbound-methods)
```python
from mock import patch
from equals import instance_of

class Dog(object):
    def bark(self):
        return 'bark'

class TestDog(TestCase):
    @mock.patch.object(Dog, 'bark', autospec=True)
    def test_dog(self, mock_bark):
        mock_bark.return_value = 'foo'

        poodle = Dog()
        poodle.haircut = 'silly'
        result = poodle.bark()

        assert result == 'foo'
        mock_bark.assert_called_once_with(poodle)
        mock_bark.assert_called_once_with(instance_of(Dog).with_attrs(haircut='silly'))
```

Quote from the illustrious @toddsifleet:
> In theory this is what equals is for, but won't work here because the instance itself is not passed into the method that validates the arguments.  (maybe that was a mistake?)
> 
> We would have to add something like `with_instance_and_args`/`with_instance_and_args_validator`, or if you only want to validate the instance `with_instance`/`with_instance_validator`.  Thoughts?

issue_title: use logging consistently, but remember the newbies
issue_body:
This issue was raised during the discussion of #95, namely that there are some places where informational messages are `print`ed directly instead of using `logging`.

I agree that it would be good to use logging consistently everywhere, but I would like to point out that I particularly **like** the usage of the `__verbose__` flag in `clients._generate.py` to `print` the file generation messages.

I am a Python newbie and haven't spent the time to figure out how to use `logging` yet, but I was able to see right away when my files were generated, which is why I followed that pattern in my pull request.

When you replace these kludges with proper logging, please set up the defaults so that these code generation messages and my munge warnings still display by default for those who don't know how to use the `logging` module (or at least provide really good, really obvious instructions).

Thanks.

3.4.5

##### Expected behavior
Is there a way with extract XML to find xml elements by its name, if name consist of at least one non letter or number, for example dast(-). 
If element name is, lets say, `<scopus>` it will locate all entities, if its `<scopus-id>` it will locate none

##### Actual behavior
please look at the attachment.
If asked to extract "scopus", which is only a part of full needed element, then we get 4 elements, but if asked to extract  "scopus-id", which is full name of element, then we get 0. I feel its because search process breaks after finding dash in between.

```xml
<reference id="1">

<reference id="2">
<sourcetitle>Acta Dermato-Venereologica</sourcetitle>

<scopus-id>0016663636</scopus-id>
<scopus-eid>2-s2.0-0016663636</scopus-eid>
</reference>
```

issue_title: Implement ability to lookup a single object using a set query
issue_body:
Like `/api/:resource/query/` but guaranteed to only return a single object or raise an error.

The motivator for this enhancement is to be able to enhance the IPAM feature set for Networks. The idea is to be able to lookup a Network object by a uniquely identifying set query, and then call a method on it (such as `next_network`) to allocate from it without ever having to know its CIDR.

e.g. from the CLI:

Currently you have to know the CIDR to be able to call `next_network`

```
$ nsot networks list -c 10.20.30.0/24 next_network -p 28
```

The idea would allow you to subsitute a CIDR w/ a set query so long as the set query can uniquely identify the network:

```
$ nsot networks list -q 'type=interconnect metro=lax' next_network -p 28
```

issue_title: Can't locate elements which with blank suffix  in content-desc
issue_body:
[criteria = locator_parts[2].strip()](https://github.com/robotframework/Selenium2Library/blob/master/src/Selenium2Library/locators/elementfinder.py#L227]) will wipe space bar off at the string end, that
lead to can't locate the special element which **with blank suffix in content-desc**. 
eg:


| Keywords  | Locator |
| ------------- | ------------- |
Mobile_Click_Element  |   accessibility_id= _some_desc_\<space bar\> |

after [strip()](https://github.com/robotframework/Selenium2Library/blob/master/src/Selenium2Library/locators/elementfinder.py#L227])'s process: 


| Keywords  | Locator |
| ------------- | ------------- |
Mobile_Click_Element  |   accessibility_id= _some_desc_ |

I've submit a PR #583 to solve this problem.
This patch using ```"``` to wrap the criteria.
If criteria has ```"``` itself, we can use accessibility_id=""value "" to deliver it's value.
Because the [[1:-1]](https://github.com/robotframework/Selenium2Library/pull/583/files#diff-b91175814577caa11b43b4be06319f57R226)  operation just strip first couple of ```"``` .

issue_title: Automate release process
issue_body:
Based on scipy 2015 talks, it would be possible and _really_ nice to automate the release process. General concept:
- Only on tagged builds
- Use issues/PRs to make release notes
- PyPI
  - Build:
    - src tarball
    - universal wheel
  - upload
- Build conda package
  - Build linux package
  - use `conda convert` to make for all platforms (since we're pure python)
  - upload

issue_title: create_filer_image_object() bug
issue_body:
I am using djangocms-helper for running tests in my project and it works very well.
The only problem is, that I have to set 'FILE_UPLOAD_TEMP_DIR' to '/tmp' (or '/tmp/') in the settings.py or the additional helper-settings. The django-doc itself says, that if FILE_UPLOAD_TEMP_DIR is set to 'None' (which is the default), it takes the default temp folder of the system (/tmp on *nix).
If you dont override this value, you get an error from the BaseTestCase in base_test.py. There, the 'create_filer_image_object()' looks at the FILE_UPLOAD_TEMP_DIR from django.conf.settings and joins it with an image_name.
If it is set to 'None', I get the following error:

>     Traceback (most recent call last):
>       File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/djangocms_helper/base_test.py", line 435, in create_filer_image_object
>         file_obj = self.create_django_image_object()
>       File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/djangocms_helper/base_test.py", line 415, in create_django_image_object
>         self.filename = os.path.join(settings.FILE_UPLOAD_TEMP_DIR, self.image_name)
>       File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/posixpath.py", line 78, in join
>         a = os.fspath(a)
>     TypeError: expected str, bytes or os.PathLike object, not NoneType

In my opinion, the method behaves different than django does itself. It would be really great if you can find a good solution for this problem.

issue_title: pipenv shell getting wrong python version
issue_body:
pipenv is installed for python3 only. I specify python3 when creating the environment in a new empty directory. `pipenv run python --version` correctly reports python3. But when I run `pipenv shell` from within that same directory, I get

```
$ python --version
Python 2.7.10
```

Here are complete steps, showing install locations and versions:

```
[shacker@shackmac dev]$ mkdir foobar
[shacker@shackmac dev]$ cd foobar
[shacker@shackmac foobar]$ which pipenv
/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.6/bin/pipenv
[shacker@shackmac foobar]$ pipenv --version
pipenv, version 2018.05.18
[shacker@shackmac foobar]$ pipenv --python 3.6
Creating a virtualenv for this project…
Using /Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.6/bin/python3.6m (3.6.3) to create virtualenv…
⠋Running virtualenv with interpreter /Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.6/bin/python3.6m
Using base prefix '/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.6'
New python executable in /Users/shacker/.local/share/virtualenvs/foobar-labP-NDG/bin/python3.6m
Also creating executable in /Users/shacker/.local/share/virtualenvs/foobar-labP-NDG/bin/python
Installing setuptools, pip, wheel...done.

Virtualenv location: /Users/shacker/.local/share/virtualenvs/foobar-labP-NDG
Creating a Pipfile for this project…
[shacker@shackmac foobar]$ pipenv run python --version
Python 3.6.3
[shacker@shackmac foobar]$ pipenv shell
Spawning environment shell (/bin/bash). Use 'exit' to leave.

bash-3.2$ . /Users/shacker/.local/share/virtualenvs/foobar-labP-NDG/bin/activate
/Users/shacker/dev/foobar
bash-3.2$ python --version
Python 2.7.10
bash-3.2$ ls -l
total 8
drwxr-xr-x   3 shacker  staff   102 May 22 08:03 ./
drwxr-xr-x  31 shacker  staff  1054 May 22 08:03 ../
-rw-r--r--   1 shacker  staff   138 May 22 08:03 Pipfile
bash-3.2$ echo $PIPENV_VENV_IN_PROJECT
[no return value]

```

This is only happening on one of my machines -- others are fine. But I can't figure out what's different about this environment to cause this. Thanks for any suggestions.

##### Steps to replicate

Given a project which has the following *Pipfile*:

```toml
[requires]
python_version = "3.6"

[packages]
pluggy = "*"
```

If this was installed before April 15th, 2018, this would have produced the following lockfile:

```json
{
    "_meta": {
        "hash": {
            "sha256": "c9c0edfb60fe650018ada97d2fd71a66171e3d74def36de484f6a156802bcc5a"
        },
        "pipfile-spec": 6,
        "requires": {
            "python_version": "3.6"
        },
        "sources": [
            {
                "name": "pypi",
                "url": "https://pypi.python.org/simple",
                "verify_ssl": true
            }
        ]
    },
    "default": {
        "pluggy": {
            "hashes": [
                "sha256:7f8ae7f5bdf75671a718d2daf0a64b7885f74510bcd98b1a0bb420eb9a9d0cff"
            ],
            "index": "pypi",
            "version": "==0.6.0"
        }
    },
    "develop": {}
}
```

The pluggy hash matches the sha value from the sdist found on https://pypi.org/project/pluggy/#files.

##### Actual result

If `pipenv sync` is run from this project now (after pluggy wheels have been published), this outputs the following warning:

```
THESE PACKAGES DO NOT MATCH THE HASHES FROM Pipfile.lock!. If you have updated the package versions, please update the hashes. Otherwise, examine the package contents carefully; someone may have tampered with them.
    pluggy==0.6.0 from https://pypi.python.org/packages/ba/65/ded3bc40bbf8d887f262f150fbe1ae6637765b5c9534bd55690ed2c0b0f7/pluggy-0.6.0-py3-none-any.whl#md5=295745cab038ef139c75aa2cdb79a5b0 (from -r /tmp/pipenv-odt4x3d3-requirements/pipenv-5t_4hr2b-requirement.txt (line 1)):
        Expected sha256 7f8ae7f5bdf75671a718d2daf0a64b7885f74510bcd98b1a0bb420eb9a9d0cff
             Got        e160a7fcf25762bb60efc7e171d4497ff1d8d2d75a3d0df7a21b76821ecbf5c5
```

It appears that pipenv downloads the newly uploaded wheel, but matches this against the known hash of the sdist.

##### Expected result

I guess pipenv should fall back to the sdist if the wheel doesn’t match any hashes.

issue_title: pipenv check can't ignore multiple vulnerabilities
issue_body:
##### Issue description

When you run `pipenv check` with multiple `--ignore` flags, it fails to pass them to `safety` tool correctly.

##### Expected result

I'd expect both vulnerabilities to be passed to `safety` correctly and thus be ignored by `safety` tool.

##### Actual result

```
$ pipenv check -i 36333 -i 36351
Checking PEP 508 requirements...
Passed!
Checking installed package safety...
Notice: Ignoring CVE(s) 36333, 36351
An error occurred:
Usage: safety check [OPTIONS]

Error: Got unexpected extra argument (36351)
```

##### Fix

This seems to be caused by the way vulnerability numbers are being passed to `safety` here:
https://github.com/pypa/pipenv/blob/master/pipenv/core.py#L2237

```python
ignored = '--ignore {0}'.format('--ignore '.join(ignore))
```
This code produces `--ignore 36333--ignore 36351` - notice lack of space after 36333!
This can be fixed by adding a space in front of join string:

```
ignored = '--ignore {0}'.format(' --ignore '.join(ignore))
                                 ^
```

##### Steps to replicate

```
$ pipenv check -i 36333 -i 36351
```

issue_title: Pipenv ignores .python-version set by pyenv
issue_body:
##### Describe your environment

1. OS Type: macOS 10.13.3
1. Python version: `$ python -V`
```
Python 2.7.13 (c925e7381036, Jun 05 2017, 20:53:58)
[PyPy 5.8.0 with GCC 4.2.1 Compatible Apple LLVM 5.1 (clang-503.0.40)]
```
1. Pipenv version: `$ pipenv --version`: pipenv, version 9.1.0

##### Expected result

```
$ pyenv local pypy2.7-5.8.0
# Creates .python-version that sets the project's python 
# version to the specific pypy version
$ pipenv install --python pypy
# Installs packages in virtualenv created with pypy2.7-5.8.0
```

##### Actual result

```
$ pipenv install --python pypy
Creating a virtualenv for this project…
Using /Users/josh/.pyenv/versions/pypy2.7-5.9.0/bin/pypy to create virtualenv…
# Installs packages in virtualenv created with pypy2.7-5.9.0, 
# or whatever the latest version of pypy2.7 I have installed is
```

I have to uninstall pypy2.7-5.9.0 and any other later versions to be able to use v5.8.0 with pipenv. I need to test with 5.8.0 specifically because that is the latest version that the heroku python buildpack supports.

Perhaps pipenv should defer to `.python-version` when choosing the python version to use, unless it conflicts with `python_version` or `python_full_version` in the `Pipfile`, in which case throw an error.

issue_title: While installing valid package, another package name is suggested.
issue_body:
I was trying to install [pymc3](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/pymc3) with pipenv. pipenv asked me whether I wanted to install `pymc` instead.

```
 % pipenv install pymc3
Did you mean pymc? [Y/n]: n
Installing pymc3…
Collecting pymc3
  Downloading pymc3-3.2-py3-none-any.whl (1.2MB)
```

I don't know if this is the intended functionality of pipenv. I can very well imagine that this is intended and meant to reduce exploitation of typos in package names. Nevertheless, in such an instance I would find it much petter if pipenv mentioned to me that it actually found pymc3, but is suggesting another package name.

##### Describe your environment

1. OS Type
MacOS (High Sierra)
1. Python version: `$ python -V`
Python 3.6.4
1. Pipenv version: `$ pipenv --version`
pipenv, version 8.3.1

##### Expected result

Either direct installation of `pymc3`, or if it is intended that asuggestion for another package is shown, a reference telling us that pymc3 exists.

issue_title: Interdependent editable packages
issue_body:
First of all, thanks for all the work on this great package!

I am working on a project updating some old packages that are not distributed via PyPI but via our own package index. Using pipenv with our own package index works great. However, in my dev environment, I'd like to install them editable from github, checking out the correct branch. I can do so by calling the `pipenv install -e git+git@github.com:<>/<>.git@<>`, which works fine.

The dependency graph of the relevant packages is as follows:

```
lizard-blockbox==0.30.dev0
  - lizard-ui [required: Any, installed: 4.40]
  - lizard-management-command-runner [required: Any, installed: 0.2.dev0]
    - lizard-ui [required: Any, installed: 4.40]
```

Pipenv version: `'11.10.1.dev4'

------------

##### Expected result

Correct installation of three packages from github.

##### Actual result

Pipenv starts installing `lizard-blockbox`, which has a dependency on `lizard-management-command-runner`. This is not on Pypi and installation fails. 

##### Steps to replicate

Generate the Pipfile as follows (the order is important -- see dependency graph):

```
pipenv --two
pipenv install django==1.8.19
pipenv install -e git+git@github.com:lizardsystem/lizard-ui.git@casper-django18#egg=lizard-ui
pipenv install -e git+git@github.com:lizardsystem/lizard-management-command-runner.git@casper-django18#egg=lizard-management-command-runner
pipenv install -e git+git@github.com:lizardsystem/lizard-blockbox.git@casper-django18#egg=lizard-blockbox
```

Resulting Pipfile
```
[[source]]
url = "https://pypi.python.org/simple"
verify_ssl = true
name = "pypi"

[packages]
django = "==1.8.19"
lizard-ui = {git = "git@github.com:lizardsystem/lizard-ui.git", editable = true, ref = "casper-django18"}
lizard-management-command-runner = {git = "git@github.com:lizardsystem/lizard-management-command-runner.git", editable = true, ref = "casper-django18"}
lizard-blockbox = {git = "git@github.com:lizardsystem/lizard-blockbox.git", editable = true, ref = "casper-django18"}

[dev-packages]

[requires]
python_version = "2.7"
```
Then remove the environment and install again: `pipenv --rm`; `pipenv --two`; `pipenv install`.

The following pip error is logged:

```
Looking in indexes: https://pypi.python.org/simple00
Obtaining lizard-blockbox from git+git@github.com:lizardsystem/lizard-blockbox.git@a85e3d9c417df52dbfb8c05ada7a7915f55aa3a5#egg=lizard-blockbox
  Skipping because already up-to-date.
Requirement already satisfied: Django<1.9,>=1.7 in ./.venv/lib/python2.7/site-packages (from lizard-blockbox) (1.8.19)
Requirement already satisfied: django-celery in ./.venv/lib/python2.7/site-packages (from lizard-blockbox) (3.2.2)
Collecting lizard-management-command-runner (from lizard-blockbox)

  Could not find a version that satisfies the requirement lizard-management-command-runner (from lizard-blockbox) (from versions: )
No matching distribution found for lizard-management-command-runner (from lizard-blockbox)
```
##### Proposed fix

A `nodeps=True` option in the Pipfile

issue_title: setuptools 36.2.2 does not allow empty 'extras_require' list
issue_body:
In one of my projects, I'm using the empty `extras_require` list syntax from https://setuptools.readthedocs.io/en/latest/setuptools.html#declaring-extras-optional-features-with-their-own-dependencies, but `setuptools` is throwing errors under 36.2.2 that weren't there in 36.2.0.

```
$ cat setup.py
from setuptools import setup

setup(
    name='testemptyextras',
    extras_require={'empty': []}
)
```

With `setuptools==36.2.0`:
```
$ pip install -e '.[empty]'
Obtaining file:///Users/amw/Desktop/setuptools
Installing collected packages: testemptyextras
  Running setup.py develop for testemptyextras
Successfully installed testemptyextras
```

However, under `setuptools==36.2.2`:
```
$ pip install -e '.[empty]'
Obtaining file:///Users/amw/Desktop/setuptools
  testemptyextras 0.0.0 does not provide the extra 'empty'
Installing collected packages: testemptyextras
  Running setup.py develop for testemptyextras
Successfully installed testemptyextras
```

issue_title: Drop support for self upgrade/installation
issue_body:
I'd like to consider dropping support for setuptools installing or upgrading itself, including the removal of the bootstrap script, and instead, rely entirely on pip for installation of setuptools. Doing so would (a) allow for the simple declaration of dependencies over vendoring them, (b) eliminate the need for a bootstrap process besides simple 'pip install', (c) eliminate the special handling for self installation, and (d) limit setuptools installation to the single-version installs that pip allows.

I expect some environments like bootstrap or other systems might be affected, so I'm cautious about this possibility. This ticket serves to host the conversation and considerations.

issue_title: Virtualenv seems to have overwritten system Python
issue_body:
Today I was trying to create a Python 2.7 virtualenv, but when I did, it replaced my Python 3.5.0 binaries with Python 2.7.10 binaries. Thereafter, I had no usable Python 3 on my system.

```
$ python3.5
Python 2.7.10 (default, Aug 22 2015, 20:33:39) 
[GCC 4.2.1 Compatible Apple LLVM 7.0.0 (clang-700.0.59.1)] on darwin
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
>>> ^D
$ which python3.5
/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.5/bin/python3.5
$ python3.5 -c "import sys; print(sys.prefix)"
/System/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/2.7
```

As you can see, I'm using OS X. I had installed Python 3.5.0 using the OS X installer from python.org. I had `alias python='python3'` in my shell. I also had originally had virtualenv installed in my Python 3.5 environment, but it wasn't working so I installed it to my Python 2.7 environment as a user package.

When I invoked virtualenv, I used something like `virtualenv --python python2.7 ../.env` where `virtualenv` probably resolved to the broken script in the Python 3 environment.

Unfortunately, I wasn't paying that much attention when it happened, so I'm unsure all the details. I'm going to try to replicate it, but for now, I just wanted to register this highly unusual and unexpected behavior.

```python
def main():

      key_name = 'ori/' + str(uuid.uuid4()) + '.png'

      tmp = path.join(prod_out, key_name)
      thumb_tmp = path.join(prod_out, key_name_thumb)

      out_img.save(tmp)
      res = post_proc(tmp, thumb_tmp, 300)

      guid = uuid.uuid1().int >> 65
      while True:
        img = db.query(Img).filter(Img.guid == guid).first()
        if not img:
          break
        guid = uuid.uuid1().int >> 65
      minio_oss = minio_oss_prefix + key_name
      minio_oss_thumb = minio_oss_prefix + key_name_thumb

      new_img = Img(guid=guid, oss=minio_oss, thumb_oss=minio_oss_thumb,
                    file_type='PNG', img_type='AI', attr=res)
      print("before upload acitve_thread {}.".format(threading.active_count()))
      upload(tmp, key_name)
      upload(thumb_tmp, key_name_thumb)
      print("after upload acitve_thread {}.".format(threading.active_count()))
      db.add(new_img)
      db.commit()
      gen.guid = guid
      gen.status = 0
      processed += 1
      db.commit()
      print("processed {}.".format(processed))
    # end for
    gens = db.query(Generation).filter(Generation.status == None).limit(
      5).all()

if __name__ == '__main__':
```

processed 107.
before upload acitve_thread 322.
after upload acitve_thread 325.
processed 108.

**problem:** the minio client will create `ThreadPool` with 3 thread (code location: minio/api.py line 1511). And these thread (minio.thread_pool.Worker) will nerver exit. issue_title: project description
issue_body:
Currently project description on github is the following:

> Parsel lets you extract text from XML/HTML documents using XPath or CSS selectors

I think it is confusing - Parsel can be used not only to extract text, it can also extract parts of XML/HTML documents with markup, or attributes of elements. 

Techniclly any part of HTML is text (including markup and tags), but I think it is confusing to use "text" in this context - usually "extract text from HTML" means "remove tags".

issue_title: KeyError when connecting to partially started cluster
issue_body:
Hi!

I have a Kafka cluster setup running 3 servers. I have a topic which has a replication-factor of 2. When I describe my topic, I see that Kafka configured my topic this way:

Topic: mytopic Partition: 0 Leader: 1 Replicas: 2,1 Isr: 1

So the topic is residing in Kafka servers 1 and 2. Then I start server 0 and 1 and I do not start server 2. In theory, mytopic is available on server 0, so I should be able to access this topic.

But when I start my application using pykafka 2.7.0, I'm getting a KeyError when calling:

kafka_client_object.topics["mytopic"]

Debugging into the topics property I see that my topic name is actually in the dictionary. Digging deeper I see that it's a custom dictionary of type TopicDict. Getting an item from this dict is calling __getitem__ function on TopicDict class. Within this function, a Topic class is instantiated:

topic = Topic(self._cluster(), meta.topics[key])

In the constructor of Topic the update function is called and that's were the error originates:

`
if meta.id not in self._partitions:
	log.debug('Adding partition %s/%s', self.name, meta.id)
	self._partitions[meta.id] = Partition(
		self, meta.id,
		brokers[meta.leader],
		[brokers[b] for b in meta.replicas],   # <---- this line
		[brokers[b] for b in meta.isr],
	)
`

For mytopic meta.replicas holds 2,1 but there are only 2 brokers connected: 0,1. So when accessing broker[2] it is throwing an KeyError.

I think the solution is to just only append brokers to this list when the broker exists, but I can't oversee if this covers the whole issue.

issue_title: incorrect authentication interface
issue_body:
Dear pyxnat

it appears that there is a problem with xnat authentication mechanism.

the mechanism asks for a username and a password, but xnat supports several user databases for
authentication purposes. The internal xnat logic fails if several databases contain same username.
As far as I can tell, this is an internal design flaw of XNAT and one cannot expect this to be fixed.

However, the HTTP FORM POST interface (which is used by web browsers) works correctly.

i suggest pyxnat implements this interface. 
Here is an example how to use it with curl

curl -c COOKIE_FILE
      --data-urlencode login_method=SERVICE \
      --data-urlencode j_username=USER   \
      --data-urlencode j_password=PASS  \
      'http://url.of.xnat.server/xnat/j_spring_security_check'

SERVICE is the name of the authentication database...

Best wishes and thank you for your help

LF

issue_title: Use "Shapely" or "sphere" for Astropy regions?
issue_body:
Should we use [Shapely](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/Shapely) and / or [Sphere](https://github.com/spacetelescope/sphere) and / or neither (i.e. re-implement or copy over things like polygon computations) as a dependency for this to-be `astropy.regions` package?

I don't remember if this important question was discussed in detail or decided at `PyAstro16`!?

Currently the API has `shapely` in many places, but nothing is really implemented and @astrofrog, I think it might just be the left-over of some API brainstorming a year or two ago?

@eteq @astrofrog @keflavich @larrybradley, all - thoughts?

If ``install_requires`` contains a package, e.g. ``pandas``, then when running:


pandas gets downloaded and installed into ``.eggs`` inside the package directory, but this is then ignored when the tests are actually run because I think we change directory to run the tests.

issue_title: bug in multichannel non-local means if channels != 3
issue_body:
## Description
The non-local means Cython code currently assumes 3 channels when computing multi-channel patch weights, but there is no check that the input images are actually 3 channel.

code that assumes three channels: [here](https://github.com/scikit-image/scikit-image/blob/cf3cdaf35797b0ebbaf155c91ecf5f8b761e0ea5/skimage/restoration/_nl_means_denoising.pyx#L108) and [here](https://github.com/scikit-image/scikit-image/blob/cf3cdaf35797b0ebbaf155c91ecf5f8b761e0ea5/skimage/restoration/_nl_means_denoising.pyx#L467-L472)
Other code outside of the weights computation [already supports arbitrary number of channels](https://github.com/scikit-image/scikit-image/blob/cf3cdaf35797b0ebbaf155c91ecf5f8b761e0ea5/skimage/restoration/_nl_means_denoising.pyx#L637)

If the input images have more than 3 channels, denoising will still occur, but the patch weights will be determined based on only the first 3 channels.  In the off chance that a two-channel image is used, out-of-bounds memory access can occur during computation of the patch weights.

The Cython code should be updated to fully support an arbitrary number of channels.  One real world use case for > 3 channels:  [hyperspectral imaging](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hyperspectral_imaging).  I volunteer to make a PR to do this.

## Way to reproduce
see the example code linked that demonstrates where the problem lies

alternatively, modify the top of the non-local means Cython code to set `boundscheck=True` and then try denoising only the first two channels of an RGB image.  You will see a lot of messages regarding out-of-bounds access on the memory-views.

issue_title: Improper computation of `centroid` in `measure._moments`

It failed:

```python
    def test_centroid():
        image = np.zeros((20, 20), dtype=np.double)

        image[15, 14] = 0.5
```

This bug was introduced in #2603.

issue_title: Remove JQuery/jcrop from Widget media
issue_body: I see that you are loading JQuery and JCrop with the form media. I wonder if this is the ideal thing to do because, I think, there is a huge possibility that JQuery (and possibly Jcrop) have already been loaded before on the template and therefore JQuery would be loaded multiples times. What do you think?, maybe saying in the docs that JQuery must be loaded before the widget would be enough (maybe saying what versions have been tested to work).

issue_title: Problem Compiling NuPIC on Mac OS 10.10.2
issue_body:
NuPIC 0.2.0 (binary) and 0.2.1.dev0 (git checkout) will not currently install/build on a Mac. The same error results using both approaches.

> What did you do? (steps to reproduce)

Setup dependencies:
[[
# First run XCode and opt to "Install Optional Components"
$ xcode-select --install # Also needed
$ sudo easy_install pip
$ sudo pip install wheel
$ sudo pip install numpy
]]

Steps to install the binary:
[[
$ sudo pip install nupic
]]

Steps to compile:
[[
$ export NUPIC=/Users/dwood/code/nupic
$ mkdir $NUPIC; cd !$
$ git clone https://github.com/numenta/nupic.git # or clone via GitHub Desktop
$ ARCHFLAGS="-arch x86_64" python setup.py install --user
]]

> What happened? (actual results)

The same error occurs in both cases:

/System/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/2.7/include/python2.7/object.h:116:56: note: expanded from macro 'Py_SIZE'
#define Py_SIZE(ob)             (((PyVarObject*)(ob))->ob_size)
                                ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^~~~~~~
_imagingft.c:237:34: error: use of undeclared identifier 'FT_GLYPH_BBOX_SUBPIXELS'
...
error: command 'cc' failed with exit status 1

> What should have happened? (expected results)

The binary should have installed, and the code should have compiled without error.

> Additional Information (operating system, python version, NuPIC commit SHA, etc.)

Mac OS X 10.10.2
Python 2.7.6 (Apple-provided system python)
NuPIC binary release 0.2.0 and GitHub source 0.2.1.dev0.

issue_title: Move model selection utilities from HTM Engine into NuPIC
issue_body:
As described by an [internal JIRA task](https://jira.numenta.com/browse/ENG-73):

Presently, unicorn's backend relies on `getScalarMetricWithTimeOfDayParams()` from htmengine, which would be better moved into NuPIC.

```python
from htmengine.algorithms.modelSelection.clusterParams import (
  getScalarMetricWithTimeOfDayParams)
```

The plan it to move the `algorithms.modelSelection*` logic and data files somewhere into nupic, where they may be shared by others:

1. move `htmengine/algorithms/modelSelection/*` to `src/nupic/frameworks/opf/common_models/`
1. Another issue is the way it loads the params from disk. https://github.com/numenta/numenta-apps/blob/master/htmengine/htmengine/algorithms/modelSelection/clusterParams.py#L80 does not work when the package is not expanded.
  - Use https://pythonhosted.org/setuptools/pkg_resources.html#resource-extraction because the package will be inside a zip file
  - or pkg_resources.resource_stream

issue_title: pip install of the windows wheel falls to install numpy and pycapnp
issue_body:
A distutils bug in Windows Python 2.7.9+ x64 assumes you will use a C++9x compiler to build source packages in the bindings\py\requirements.txt file. And not the appropriate and necessary MinGW GCC tool-chain.

The following changes in the requirements.txt file can exclude packages that require external wheel installation -
> numpy==1.9.2; 'linux' in sys_platform
> pycapnp==0.5.5; 'linux' in sys_platform

Possibly also requiring -
> pip>=7.1.2
> distribute>=0.7.3

issue_title: RFC Admin interface bootstrap template
issue_body:
**Problem**

Currently our admin interfaces is constrained in space (e.g. tables gets squeeze and there's not much real estate), and design is not particularly nice.

**Proposal**
1. Change base templates for all pages below ``/admin/`` that uses Flask-Admin to a standard admin bootstrap theme.
2. Here are examples of free templates:
  - http://almsaeedstudio.com
  - http://www.blacktie.co/2014/07/dashgum-free-dashboard/
  - http://www.egrappler.com/templatevamp-free-twitter-bootstrap-admin-template/
  - http://startbootstrap.com/template-overviews/sb-admin-2/

Alternatively we could build something ourselves and model it over something like:
http://demo.neontheme.com/dashboard/main-3/

issue_title: WebSubmit: `pdfopt` to be replaced for PDF linearization when Ghostscript >= v9.0.7
issue_labels: [{'id': 96318385, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/inveniosoftware/invenio/labels/Branch:%20maint-1.0', 'name': 'Branch: maint-1.0', 'color': 'd4c5f9', 'default': False}, {'id': 96238698, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/inveniosoftware/invenio/labels/Component:%20WebSubmit', 'name': 'Component: WebSubmit', 'color': 'ededed', 'default': False}, {'id': 96237777, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/inveniosoftware/invenio/labels/Type:%20bug', 'name': 'Type: bug', 'color': 'ff2a00', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
Originally on 2013-07-18

PDF can be _linearized_ (for fast web viewing) using `pdfopt` in `websubmit_file_converter.py`. The `pdfopt` tool used to be part of Ghostscript prior to version 9.0.7 and [has now been removed](http://git.ghostscript.com/?p=ghostpdl.git;a=commitdiff;h=260ddb3e53ffe852a8994dd00719931ca3f2beec). 
It would be adequate to use directly Ghostscript for linearizing PDF when Ghostscript version > 9.0.7 is detected (as `pdfwrite` will  [allow linearization](http://git.ghostscript.com/?p=ghostpdl.git;a=commitdiff;h=f2a40a3a7edd6d27caeaa19f7f644adf31ee972c) if `-dFastWebView` parameter [is set](http://www.ghostscript.com/doc/9.07/Ps2pdf.htm).

If the code is not updated the linearization will still be attempted but will fail, due to #1560.

issue_title: dictdiffer breaks if dict contains numpy arrays
issue_body:
When comparing two nested dictionaries containing numpy arrays it breaks with the well known:
```
ValueError: The truth value of an array with more than one element is ambiguous. Use a.any() or a.all()
```
To reproduce:

```
import numpy as np
from dictdiffer import diff

d1 = {'a': np.array([1, 2, 3])}
d2 = {'a': np.array([1, 2, 4])}

print(list(diff(d1,d2)))
```
Traceback:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "example.py", line 7, in <module>
    print(list(diff(d1,d2)))
  File "/local/kai/anaconda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/dictdiffer/__init__.py", line 150, in diff
    for diffed in recurred:
  File "/local/kai/anaconda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/dictdiffer/__init__.py", line 205, in diff
    if are_different(first, second, tolerance):
  File "/local/kai/anaconda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/dictdiffer/utils.py", line 264, in are_different
    if first == second:
ValueError: The truth value of an array with more than one element is ambiguous. Use a.any() or a.all()
```

Unfortunately I have no elegant solution to this, but changing `are_different` like this works for my specific use case:

```
    # only check if same type
    if type(first) == type(second):
        # check if ndarray type
        if 'ndarray' in str(type(first)):
            return not (first == second).all()
        if first == second:
            # values are same - simple case
            return False
        elif bool(first != first) ^ bool(second != second):
            # only one of them is 'NaN', hence they are different
            return True
        elif isinstance(first, num_types) and isinstance(second, num_types):
            # (a) two numerical values are compared with tolerance
            # (b) both values are NaN and they will never fit the tolerance
            return abs(first-second) > tolerance * max(abs(first), abs(second))
    # we got different values
    return True
```

issue_title: Add ability to include the hostname in lockfile in addition to the PID
issue_body:
In a container environment (e.g. Docker), the PID is typically always identical even if multiple containers are running under the same operating system instance.

Clearly, inspecting lock files doesn't then help much in debugging. It would be very useful to be able to identify the container which created the lock file. Since Docker uses the container identifier or name as the hostname, this information can be stored in the lock file in addition to or instead of the PID.

issue_title: Avoid jamming a thread on blocking push to ringbuffer
issue_body: The change to a blocking push on the ringbuffer means that a slow consumer can now jam up an asio thread. Consider changing the push to be asynchronous with asio calling back when the consumer has caught up. This will introduce complexity and possibly reduce performance, so needs to be carefully considered.

issue_title: MesosOperatorMasterDriver cannot subscribe to events
issue_body:
Using the latest version of PyMesos 0.3.4, I cannot receive any events using the operator API. It looks like the SUBSCRIBE request is not sent, from my initial investigation.

On PyMesos 0.3.3, after calling `driver.start()`, I get a log message similar to the following:

```
Operator client subscribed with cluster state: ...
```

However, on 0.3.4, no such log is present, and any task updates or agent updates will not notify the operator.

I believe this can be reproduced with the basic examples provided. Falling back to PyMesos 0.3.3 fixes this issue, which seems like #98 introduced a regression.

issue_title: Issue withSignature Regex
issue_body:
There appears to be an issue with the original regex defined for matching signatures:
(--|__|-\w)|(^Sent from my (\w+\s*){1,3})

The above correctly matches the phrase: 'Sent from my iPhone' etc.

But the first group also matches any line containing '--', '__' or '-'.  So you get matches for any line containing a hyphenated word for instance:
'This is an auto-reply test to see if I receive an automated message'

I've tested this using the online tool at https://regex101.com/ and also using the built-in .Net regular expression matcher.

The fix appears to be to ensure the first group only looks for matches that start with the defined characters:
(^--|^__|^-\w)|(^Sent from my (\w+\s*){1,3})

issue_title: Math errors in version 2.4.4 in Windows 10
issue_body:
I'm getting bizarre and random mathematical errors when running version 2.4.4 on a Windows 10 machine. For example, `exp(n)` is yielding *negative numbers* , or a call to `pandas.eval()` on a boolean expression will yield all False. Suffice to say, it is critically broken. What is even more frustrating is that the problem occurs randomly: I will run my program and it will crash in an early step; when I re-run it again, it will crash in a completely different step. 

I haven't had much luck reproducing the problem from a Notebook environment, however I have access to two Windows 10 machines and they both exhibited the same behaviour running the exact same code on the exact same version of `numexpr`. When I downgrade to Version 2.4.3, the problem completely goes away. Furthermore, version 2.4.4 works just fine on my Windows 7 machine, so this appears to be specific to Win 10. 

Any help would be much appreciated.

issue_title: Nominatim: view_box parameter for Nominatim instance only works with string-represented numerical tuple
issue_body:
Hi and thanks for all your collective work on geopy. Really appreciate it and super helpful for data analytics! 

(Note, as an aside, that it's a little weird that the tuple is (longitude1, latitude1, longitude2, latitude2) instead of leading each 2-element pair within the 4-element tuple with latitude, but that seems to be [Nominatim's convention](https://github.com/openstreetmap/Nominatim/blob/81a7ea36db7d3b055acf25a36d2ea2c27af74765/test/bdd/api/search/params.feature), so it makes sense to keep it that way for the Class's parameters in geopy too - so that's not what this issue is about.)

Anyhow, the view_box parameter is a 4-element tuple of positive or negative integer or floating point data defining opposite corners of a rectangle in a 4-element python tuple(). If you use numerical data to create the tuple, you receive an error:

Setup and Call: 
```
box_tuple = tuple([180, 22, -180, -22])
geolocator = Nominatim(view_box=box_tuple)
location2 = geolocator.geocode("Peru")
```
 
Error:
```
~/anaconda3/lib/python3.6/site-packages/geopy/geocoders/osm.py in geocode(self, query, exactly_one, timeout, addressdetails, language, geometry)
    161         # `viewbox` apparently replaces `view_box`
    162         if self.view_box:
--> 163             params['viewbox'] = ','.join(self.view_box)
    164 
    165         if self.country_bias:

TypeError: sequence item 0: expected str instance, int found
```

If you use string data to create the tuple, no error, and the call to geocode() works as expected, resulting in a location returned that lies within the defined boundaries.

Setup and Call: 
```
box_tuple = tuple(["180", "22", "-180", "-22"])
geolocator = Nominatim(view_box=box_tuple)
location2 = geolocator.geocode("Peru")
location2
```

Output:
`Location(Perú, (-6.8699697, -75.0458515, 0.0))`

FWIW, my suggested fix would be to convert a numerical tuple to strings somehow, but I am not sure what the most efficient implementation or input handling would be, which is why I'm submitting this as an issue instead of coding an implementation.

I'm using geopy 1.11.0 with a Python 3.6.2 in a jupyter notebook.

Please let me know if I've been unclear or you need more info or have other questions. Thanks again, so much for all your work on this.

issue_title: Trade at the Close vs Trade at the Open
issue_body:
The common workflow for `alphalens` as described [here](https://www.quantopian.com/lectures/factor-analysis) is

- take a `results` dataframe as output from `run_pipeline`
- get pricing as

```
assets = results.index.levels[1]
pricing = get_pricing(
    assets,
    pd.Timestamp(start),
    pd.Timestamp(end) + pd.Timedelta(days=30),
    fields="open_price"
)
```

- combine the `results` and `pricing` dataframes as

```
factor_data[factor] = al.utils.get_clean_factor_and_forward_returns(
    factor=results[factor],
    prices=pricing,
    periods=[1]
)
```
- perform whatever analysis you like, e.g.,
```
ls = al.performance.factor_returns(factor_data[factor])
```

This workflow (rightly) pre-supposes that the "information" arrives prior to the market open and the first opportunity to capture that information is by trading benchmarked to the market open.

However, when working with, for example, a factor based purely on price and volume data, it might be desirable to analyze the factor against the contemporaneous closing price, **not against the next day's open price**. To give a concrete example, if I have a daily mean-reversion factor that uses the close price on day `t` to generate a signal, I want to measure it **assuming execution at the close price on** `t`. The spirit of what I am hoping to analyze is: in real production trading, one might generate the signal at 3:50pm and trade benchmarked to the 4pm close price. Forgetting any arguments as to the philosophical reasons why one should not do this for analysis or whether it is practical to do in production trading please, my question is:

What is the most elegant way to modify the above workflow to use the close_price for `t` for the `alphalens` studies? Noting the obvious that simply changing `fields="open_price"` to `fields="close_price` would result in `alphalens` studies versus the `t+1` close price, not the `t` close price.

In the industry, this would be called a "delay 0" alpha, versus the workflow above which would be called a "delay 1" alpha.

issue_title: [Support Request] How do I silence the extra copy of django 500 errors that Raven is producing?
issue_body:
My boss asked me to integrate Sentry logging into our django projects, and I was pleasantly surprised by the sheer ease of installation. However, I'm now frustrated because I'm getting 2 Sentry events for every 500 error, and I don't know how to silence the one I don't want.

What appears to be happening is that Sentry is logging every exception that my app throws, even when Sentry hasn't been configured in the `LOGGING` dictionary. So when I *do* configure it in `LOGGING` (I only want it to log errors from the `django.request` logger), I get two Sentry events: one for the raw exception, and one for the 500 error sent to `django.request`. Here's what that looks like in my Sentry logs: http://i.imgur.com/QuruFZZ.png

I want to remove the one that doesn't have a custom tag. How do I do that? I've tried setting every logger I could find in the Raven codebase to the 'null' logging handler, but that doesn't seem to help.

LDFLAGS=" -Wl,-rpath=${MKLROOT}/lib/intel64_lin -L${MKLROOT}/lib/intel64_lin -lmkl_intel_lp64 -lmkl_core -lmkl_sequential -lpthread -lm -ldl "
How can I achieve this in scons?

Thanks,

Wim

issue_title: Add a function to load a wavefront map from outside
issue_body:
It would be nice to add a function to load a wavefront map from outside such as ZEMAX. The first target would be to load an output file of "Wavefront Map" in ZEMAX, which will allow us to simulate more realistic optical PSF.

ZEMAX can output a wavefront map, which divides a pupil plane into a power of 2 (from 32 x 32 to 8192 x 8192), for a position of FOV. So we need to interpolate both on pupil plane and FOV.

As a first step, it might be nice to write up a simple draft version of this as "WavefrontPSF" or similar, without interpolations above.

issue_title: The git tags newest version on GitHub repository is different from website
issue_body:
Hi Font-Awesome team members,
I'm a member of [cdnjs](https://github.com/cdnjs/cdnjs). We hosted this [library](https://github.com/cdnjs/cdnjs/tree/master/ajax/libs/font-awesome).

I saw the library release v5.0.1 & v5.0.0 on the [website](https://fontawesome.com/how-to-use/upgrading-from-4), such as `https://use.fontawesome.com/releases/v5.0.0/js/all.js`.
But there is no v5.0.0 and v5.0.1 of git tag from GitHub repo.
The git auto-updater in [cdnjs](https://github.com/cdnjs/cdnjs) relies on [**git tags**](https://github.com/FortAwesome/Font-Awesome/releases) so that it can recognize the correct version and automatically update the repo.

Would you mind adding git tag or telling me your new git repository that CDNJS's bot can automatically add the new versions of this awesome library?

Sorry for bothering you.
If you have any questions or concerns, please let me know.

Many thanks.

cdnjs/cdnjs#12307

issue_title: Patroni died unexpectedly due to vulnerability scanner

We're planning to migrate to Patroni. Yesterday morning, every member of our patroni test cluster died with something like the following error:
Jan 22 10:34:49 psql-test03 patroni[29974]: ----------------------------------------
Jan 22 10:34:49 psql-test03 patroni[29974]: Exception happened during processing of request from ('192.168.1.88', 53234)
Jan 22 10:34:49 psql-test03 patroni[29974]: ----------------------------------------

That IP address corresponds to our vulnerability scanner. My best guess is that the vulnerability scanner found this patroni API port and started sending garbage to it to try to see if there were any known exploits. Unfortunately it seems to have caused all three members of the cluster to crash.

This is the only information in the logs. Is there a way to increase logging verbosity to see exactly where/how it died?
